title: Director, Training and Development
description:
**Company Overview**



Immunome is a clinical\-stage targeted oncology company committed to developing first\-in\-class and best\-in\-class targeted cancer therapies. We are advancing an innovative portfolio of therapeutics, drawing on leadership that previously played key roles in the design, development, and commercialization of cutting\-edge therapies, including antibody\-drug conjugate therapies. Our pipeline includes varegacestat, a late\-clinical stage GSI; IM\-1021, a clinical\-stage ROR1 ADC; and IM\-3050, a FAP\-targeted radiotherapy that recently received IND clearance. We are also advancing a broad portfolio of early stage ADCs pursuing undisclosed solid tumor targets.

**Position Overview**



Immunome is building the commercial capabilities required to prepare for launch and scale a high\-performing field organization. The Director, Training \& Development will own the strategy, roadmap, and operating model for commercial training across Sales, Marketing, and other customer\-facing roles. This leader will design and deliver launch curricula, scalable onboarding, coaching and manager toolkits, and a continuous learning engine—while ensuring training governance, compliance, and medical accuracy in close partnership with Medical Affairs and Compliance. This person will also design and deliver medical field training curricula.



In addition, this role will lead commercial development programming that supports people development across all Commercial functions. The Director will partner closely with HR to build and support all\-organization development initiatives, positioning Commercial to lead the way in designing and delivering high\-quality, impactful, and relevant development experiences.


**Responsibilities**


**Commercial Training Leadership**


* Develop and own Immunome's commercial training strategy, roadmap, and operating model for field teams (Sales, Marketing, and customer\-facing roles).
* Build scalable onboarding and continuous learning programs aligned to brand strategy, field competencies, and organizational priorities.
* Design, develop, and deliver launch curricula for Field Sales and field customer\-facing medical roles (as applicable), including selling skills, market landscape, competitive insights, objection handling, and customer engagement excellence.
* Create an all\-commercial competency model (role\-based expectations, proficiency levels, and assessment approach) to support readiness, development, and performance coaching.
* Establish and maintain a Sales Manager coaching framework, including manager toolkits, observation guides, field coaching routines, and reinforcement mechanisms.


**Learning Infrastructure, LMS \& Scalable Enablement**


* Implement and evolve training infrastructure, including learning platforms/LMS, SOPs, templates, and scalable content management processes.
* Build role\-based learning pathways and certification journeys that support launch readiness and ongoing development.
* Leverage modern enablement approaches (microlearning, blended learning, reinforcement, and knowledge checks) to improve retention and field application.


**Launch Meetings, Workshops \& Field Readiness Events**


* Lead the strategy, planning, and execution of launch meetings, National Sales Meetings (NSM), and cross\-functional field training events.
* Oversee agenda design, applied learning experiences, content development, vendor coordination, and onsite execution.
* Design and deliver launch meeting workshops that translate brand strategy into field\-ready execution, including message pull\-through and scenario practice.


**Training Measurement, Evaluation \& Continuous Improvement**


* Establish an evaluation strategy with dashboards, KPIs, and feedback loops to measure training effectiveness and readiness impact (completion, certification, proficiency, and field adoption).
* Partner with Sales Leadership and commercial stakeholders to assess performance gaps and deploy targeted learning solutions.
* Drive continuous improvement by incorporating field insights, performance data, and stakeholder feedback into training updates and reinforcement.


**AI\-Enabled Training Innovation**


* Champion responsible use of AI\-enabled tools to accelerate content creation, streamline updates, and increase scalability while maintaining quality, compliance, and medical accuracy.
* Establish governance and best practices for compliant AI use across training content and delivery.
* Evaluate emerging learning technologies and integrate tools that improve speed\-to\-readiness and knowledge retention.


**Commercial Development**


* Oversee the design, implementation, and management of commercial development programs and projects to support all functions within Commercial.
* Build and maintain development pathways that support skill growth and career progression across Commercial roles, using a variety of development approaches (e.g., workshops, cohorts, coaching, self\-paced learning, experiential assignments, and peer learning).
* Partner with Commercial leaders to assess development needs and prioritize initiatives that strengthen capabilities, leadership, and organizational effectiveness.
* Partner with HR to build and support all\-organization development initiatives, ensuring Commercial leads the way in developing and delivering high\-quality, impactful, and relevant development experiences.


**Medical Training (Disease State \& Product Only)**


* In partnership with Medical Affairs, develop training modules focused on disease state, epidemiology, mechanism of action, clinical data, safety, and patient journey.
* Ensure medical training content is aligned with approved scientific resources and remains within non\-promotional boundaries.
* Design and support implementation of medical field on\-boarding encompassing all aspects of required medical on\-boarding needs.


**Cross\-Functional Leadership, Governance \& Compliance**


* Partner closely with Commercial, Medical Affairs, HR, Legal/Regulatory, and Compliance to ensure training content and national meeting materials meet applicable standards.
* Oversee training governance and compliance processes (e.g., content review workflows, certification standards, documentation practices) in alignment with internal requirements.
* Build and manage relationships with external agencies, instructional designers, and meeting vendors; manage budgets, scopes of work, and performance expectations.


**Qualifications**


* Bachelor's degree required; advanced degree preferred.
* A minimum of 10 years of experience in biotech/pharmaceutical commercial training, medical training, and/or commercial operations.
* Proven experience building or scaling training functions and leading national meetings in a high\-growth or launch\-ready organization.


**Knowledge and Skills**


* Experience designing and delivering development programs and pathways that support skill building and career growth; ability to partner effectively with HR and cross\-functional stakeholders.
* Demonstrated ability to translate complex clinical and strategic content into clear, engaging, role\-based learning experiences.
* Experience partnering with Medical Affairs and Compliance to deliver medically accurate, compliant training content.
* Strong project management, communication, and stakeholder alignment skills; comfort operating in a fast\-paced, highly collaborative environment.
* Vendor and budget management experience.
* Strategic and execution\-oriented leader with a builder mindset and comfort operating with ambiguity.
* Scientific fluency and the ability to simplify complex concepts for field application.
* Strong facilitation skills; credible presence with senior commercial leaders and field audiences.
* Deep understanding of commercial onboarding, selling skills, coaching, and competency frameworks.
* Demonstrated capability to design and scale people development programs and create role\-based development pathways.
* Fluency in learning measurement approaches (KPIs, dashboards, readiness metrics) and continuous improvement methodologies.

 **Qualifications*** Bachelor’s degree in business, marketing, or a related field; additional certifications in training and development are a plus.

title: HR Manager
description:
**Be a People \& Systems Leader at a Mission‑Driven Healthcare Organization**



Are you an HR professional ready to step into a management role where your leadership, systems thinking, and interpersonal strengths can shine? Wallace is seeking an HR Manager to manage the daily HR operations and serve as the primary resource for staff and leaders. This role is ideal for an experienced HR professional who’s ready to take full ownership of HR processes, strengthen systems, and support a mission‑driven team.

  


**Key Details:**


* **Compensation:** $80,000\-$90,000 per year, DOE
* **Schedule:** Full time, Monday\-Friday, with four days onsite and one remote day after onboarding
* **Location:** 18633 SE Stark St., Portland, OR 97233
* **Benefits:** Medical, Dental \& Vision Insurance (Wallace covers employee premiums 100%); PTO (three weeks in the first year, four weeks starting in year two); Employer paid Short and Long‑Term Disability; Life \& AD\&D; FSA/DCFSA; 401(k) with match; Employee Assistance Program.

  


**About Us:** At Wallace, we're on a mission to improve the health outcomes of our community. As a not for profit Federally Qualified Health Center, we've been a stable and trusted healthcare provider for over 40 years. Founded by Dr. James Reuler, Wallace began by bringing essential medical care to uninsured and low\-income residents. Today, we offer comprehensive medical, dental, and behavioral health services, staying deeply connected to our vibrant, multicultural community. We stay deeply connected to our multicultural community and believe in supporting the whole person, including our staff.

  


**Why You’ll Love This Role:**


* **Hands‑On HR Leadership:** Step confidently into HR management with full responsibility for day to day HR work.
* **Systems Ownership:** Build and maintain strong HR processes, documentation, and workflows.
* **People Centered Environment:** Work in a mission‑driven culture that values communication, clarity, and collaboration.
* **Variety \& Impact:** Influence key aspects of employee experience, culture, and organizational success.
* **No Weekends!** Stable Monday\-Friday schedule.

  


**What You’ll Do:**


**HR Leadership \& Compliance**


* Serve as the primary HR point of contact for employees and managers.
* Manage day to day HR operations, documentation, and compliance with an emphasis on building and maintaining efficient systems.
* Interpret and apply employment laws, policies, and HR best practices, offering clear guidance grounded in both organizational needs and legal requirements.
* Partner with leadership and external HR support on complex or sensitive matters.


**Employee Relations \& Culture**


* Build trusting, transparent relationships with staff across the organization, maintaining a steady, calm presence in challenging conversations.
* Provide direct support, coaching, and clarity to leaders and employees, addressing needs while upholding policies and boundaries.
* Lead employee relations work including performance conversations, conflict resolution, and navigating interpersonal dynamics in a community health environment.
* Help cultivate a positive, inclusive, and supportive workplace culture with a proactive interpersonal approach.


**Core HR Operations**


* Manage benefits administration and open enrollment, acting as the primary contact with brokers and internal stakeholders.
* Oversee leave administration, ADA accommodations, and offboarding with clarity, consistency, and care.
* Maintain personnel files, records, and HR communication workflows with strong attention to structure, organization, and system efficiency.
* Coordinate with payroll and admin support on timecards, credentialing, and compliance requirements.
* Lead recruitment and hiring processes, including sourcing mission aligned candidates and coordinating a smooth interview experience.
* Deliver an effective onboarding experience and oversee performance management cycles, including reviews and Personnel Change Notices.

  


**What We’re Looking For:**


* Bachelor’s degree in business, HR, or related field or equivalent experience preferred.
* 3 \- 5 years of progressive HR experience, demonstrating increased responsibility and readiness to step into management.
* Strong systems, organization, and process‑development skills.
* Excellent interpersonal, communication, and relationship‑building abilities.
* Capacity to work independently and bring clarity, structure, and follow‑through.
* Solid understanding of HR compliance and employment laws.
* Experience in healthcare or ADP is helpful but not required.
* PHR or SHRM‑CP is a plus.

  


*Wallace is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We believe in the power of community health. We value diversity, inclusion, and the strength that comes from serving a vibrant community. We encourage candidates from all backgrounds to apply.*

title: Human Resources Coordinator
company: Park Chenaur and Associates, Inc., P.S.
description:

title: Senior Human Resources Generalist
description:
**Position Overview:**

The Senior HR Generalist is a people\-focused operator who helps ensure we consistently have the Right People in the Right Seats by executing our people processes with clarity and consistency. This role is the primary owner of day\-to\-day HR administration, compliance execution, employee lifecycle processes, and people data integrity. The Senior HR Generalist ensures our recruiting, onboarding, training, documentation, and offboarding processes are documented, followed\-by\-all, and compliant. Partnering closely with the President and outsourced HR resources, this role supports role clarity, accountability, and a positive employee experience while maintaining accurate, audit\-ready people systems.

**Key Responsibilities include but are not limited**

* Own the day\-to\-day execution of HR processes across the employee lifecycle, ensuring they are documented, followed\-by\-all, and compliant
* Serve as the primary owner of HR administration, employee records, HRIS data integrity, and people documentation.
* Maintain HR workflows, checklists, and systems to support consistent, audit\-ready processes.
* Execute full\-cycle recruiting and hiring logistics, ensuring alignment with Core Values and

GWC.

* Execute onboarding and offboarding using documented processes, including I\-9s, background checks, HRIS setup, system access changes, and Day 1 readiness.

**Knowledge, Skills and Abilities:**

* Effective communicator with employees, managers, and external partners.

Benefits:

* Flexible schedule

title: Principal, Employee Listening & Sensing
description:
**WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH**
------------------------


This role sits within NIKE’s global People Analytics \& Insights (PAI) team known for its analytical rigor, storytelling excellence, and deep partnership across HR and the global enterprise. The Principal, Employee Listening \& Sensing serves as a thought leader and subject matter expert in the Employee Listening function, partners closely across PAI, and collaborates with key partners within HR (e.g., HRBP, Talent COE) and stakeholders outside of HR (e.g., Legal/Privacy, Communications, business units).

--------------------------


We are looking for a strategic thinker who blends research expertise, analytical depth, and storytelling craft. This ideal individual thrives in complexity, translating data into insight and insight into action. They excel at mixed‑methods analysis, stakeholder partnership, and navigating ambiguity with confidence. This ideal candidate elevates employee voice, influences senior leaders, and pushes the Employee Listening discipline forward with bold thinking and inclusive leadership. They bring curiosity, precision, and a commitment to delivering insights that drive real impact for teammates across the globe.

-----------------------

title: Human Resources Specialist
description:
Overview:

Provides clerical and administrative support to the Human Resources Department on all personnel and HR matters. Provides prompt customer services and communications to internal and external individuals. Requirements for this job change frequently with changing priorities.


Responsibilities:
* Perform customer service functions by responding to employee requests and questions via phone, email, or in person.

* Creating and running reports using ADP.

* Process educational reimbursements.
* Create sea time letters and random drug testing letters for maritime employees.

* Exceptional verbal, written and interpersonal communication skills are a must along with a positive, can\-do attitude.

* Work with computers, fax machines, copiers, mail/postage machines, telephones.

Expected Hours of Work:
* Normal office hours are 8:00 a.m. to 5:00 p.m. Monday through Thursday. Friday can be flexible in\-office or remote, however it may be required to be in office on Fridays, as needed.

Company Overview:

Centerline Logistics is a leading provider of marine transportation services in the United States. Centerline Logistics operates on the United States West Coast (including Alaska and Hawaii), United States East Coast (including Puerto Rico), the United States Gulf Coast and the Mississippi River System. Delta Hotels by Marriott Seattle Everett is seeking an organized and experienced HR Coordinator to support human resources operations at our beautiful 232\-room, full\-service hotel.
  

This position plays a key role in maintaining structure, consistency, and professionalism across all HR processes. The HR Coordinator works closely with the General Manager and Corporate HR to support recruitment, onboarding, employee relations, compliance, and day\-to\-day HR administration across multiple departments including Rooms, Food \& Beverage, Banquets, Sales, and Engineering.
**THE ROLE**
The HR Coordinator supports core HR functions at the property level, ensuring processes are handled accurately and consistently. This includes recruitment coordination, onboarding, benefits administration, record\-keeping, and compliance tracking.
  

The role also assists the General Manager and department leaders with coaching and corrective action processes, helping ensure documentation is clear, consistent, and aligned with company standards. The HR Coordinator partners with Corporate HR on more complex employee relations matters as needed.
  

This position serves as a trusted resource for managers and employees regarding policies, benefits, and procedures while maintaining appropriate professional boundaries in a fast\-paced hotel environment.
**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES**
* Coordinate recruitment and onboarding for all hotel departments
* Maintain accurate, confidential personnel files and HRIS records
* Administer benefits enrollment and respond to employee benefit inquiries
* Support hotel safety program and workers’ compensation return\-to\-work process.
* Respond to unemployment requests and attend unemployment hearings.
* Track compliance requirements, training records, and required postings
* Support managers with performance documentation and corrective action processes
* Ensure completion of required company and brand training and ongoing employee engagement.
* Review written documentation for clarity and policy alignment
* Assist with employee relations matters and escalate when appropriate
* Ensure consistent application of company policies and employment practices
**WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR**
* Strong organizational skills and attention to detail
* Experience supporting coaching and performance management processes
* Ability to review documentation for clarity and consistency
* Professional discretion when handling confidential matters
* Clear communicator who works well with both managers and employees
* Comfortable operating in a high\-volume, multi\-department hotel environment

**QUALIFICATIONS**
* 2–4 years of HR experience, preferably in hospitality or multi\-department operations
* Associate degree in HR, Business Administration, or related field
* Working knowledge of employment law and HR best practices
* Experience with HRIS systems and Microsoft Office; ADP Workforce Now preferred
* Ability to work a minimum of 32 hours per week, ideally across five days

**PHYSICAL REQUIREMENTS**
This work requires the ability to sit for long periods of time and computer use, with occasional walking, bending, reaching, or carrying light items for filing, training, or safety inspections. Hand and finger dexterity is necessary for typing, writing, and other routine office tasks. Visual acuity is required to read documents, forms, and computer screens accurately. Auditory ability must allow effective communication in person, by telephone, and in group settings, even with background noise.

**SCHEDULE:**
* On\-site, Monday \- Friday
* 32\-40 hours per week

**RATE OF PAY:**
* $23\.54 to $26\.06 per hour/DOE

**BENEFITS AND PERKS:**
* Paid Time Off
* Holiday Pay
* Paycheck Advancement Program
* Hotel Travel Discounts
* Supplemental Insurance
* Employee Assistance Program
* Employee Meals

For eligible employees:
* Insurance \- Medical, Dental, Vision, Life and AD\&D
* 401(k) Plan with Employer Match


  

**Drug Free Workplace, Equal Opportunity, E\-Verify Employer**

title: Orchard HR/H2A Manager
description:
**COMPANY OVERVIEW** 

Legendary Fruit Company is a vertically integrated tree fruit (apples, pears, and cherries) grower, packer and distributor based in Chelan, Manson and Wapato, Washington. Through a series of acquisitions, we have assembled one of the largest tree fruit platforms in the country with proprietary varieties, regional diversification, and a large quantity of fruit.

We passionately share the awesomeness of apples, pears and cherries with everyone, everywhere. Our vision is to be the most trusted partner in the fruit industry, known for our legendary people, farms, growers and products.

**Job Duties and Responsibilities:**

· Partner with managers and supervisors to implement and uphold HR policies, procedures, and best practices.

· Communicate policy updates and coordinate required training and educational initiatives.

· Manage H\-2A contracts and support temporary worker housing in coordination with Orchard operations.

· Supervise Orchard HR team members.

· Administer the workers’ compensation program, including new claims and ongoing case management.

· Oversee employee training programs for orchard and warehouse operations.

· Manage employee benefits administration, including enrollments, changes, and employee support.

· Lead recruitment, sourcing, and onboarding efforts in collaboration with leadership.

· Administer the full employee lifecycle, from onboarding through offboarding.

· Process unemployment claims and employment verification requests.

· Complete annual regulatory compliance filings.

· Collaborate with the Director of Human Resources and Warehouse General Manager on policies, procedures, and employee\-related matters.

· Perform additional duties as assigned.

**Education \& Experience:** 

* Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources or a related field required. Equivalent related experience may be considered in lieu of formal education.
* Minimum of five (5\) years of progressive Human Resources experience required.
* Bilingual proficiency in Spanish required.
* Valid driver’s license required.
* Valid transportation required.
* Experience with Paylocity HRIS system preferred.
* Experience with GANAZ onboarding system preferred.

**Physical Requirements/Knowledge/Skills/Abilities:**

· Prolonged periods of sitting at a desk and working on a computer.

· Must be able to access and navigate each department at the organization’s facilities.

· Ability to write, communicate, understand, remember, and apply oral and/or written instructions or other information in English and Spanish.

· Ability to plan, organize, and carry out assignments from management staff with minimal direction.

· Ability to analyze situations, interpret data and make decisions that have moderate impact on immediate work unit.

· Ability to organize and prioritize job duties in an effective and timely manner, set priorities and meet critical deadlines.

· Ability to maintain accurate files and records.

· Physical requirements for this position include hearing, seeing, speaking, feeling, reaching, pinching, and repetitive motions.

· This position requires standing and walking for long periods of time.

· This position requires exerting up to 20 pounds of force in order to lift, carry, pull, or move objects.

Full Benefit Package Includes:

Medical, Dental, Vision, Long Term Disability, Life Insurance, 401k, 401k match, paid holidays, paid vacation and paid sick leave.

Job Type: Full\-time

Pay: $100,000\.00 \- $110,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k) matching

* Flexible spending account
* Health insurance

title: Human Resources - Specialist
description:
Why Join Our HR Team?
  
We are growing and looking for someone who is passionate about taking care of people and putting employees first.
  
Our HR team is collaborative, proactive, and deeply committed to doing things the right way. We partner closely with leaders, support employees through important moments in their careers, and play a key role in shaping the culture of our organization. This is an opportunity to contribute to a team that supports our people and helps strengthen our culture every day.
  
We are seeking someone who thrives in a fast\-paced environment, handles confidential information with professionalism and discretion, recruits top talent, and is ready to contribute in a meaningful way every day.
  
If you are dependable, people\-focused, and excited to grow with a team that leads with integrity and shows up for one another, we encourage you to apply today!
  
Summary
  
The HR Specialist will partner closely with the HR team to support recruiting efforts, benefits administration, compliance, and day to day HR operations. This position requires strong judgment, discretion, and the ability to manage sensitive matters with professionalism and care.
  
  

Key Responsibilities
  
* Serve as a primary point of contact for employees regarding benefits, policies, time off, and general HR inquiries
* Maintain and audit personnel records and HRIS data to ensure accuracy, confidentiality, and compliance
* Support full cycle recruiting and onboarding across multiple offices and divisions
* Support company compliance program efforts across all locations
* Partner with leadership and HR team members on employee appreciation initiatives and employee care programs
* Provide guidance and support on escalated employee matters, including policy interpretation, corrective action, and ensuring consistent application of company standards
* Assist with workers compensation administration, leave management, and return to work coordination
* Ensure compliance with federal, state, and local employment laws and company policies


Qualifications
  
* Minimum of 2 years of progressive HR experience, including employee relations and workers compensation administration
* Working knowledge of federal and multi state labor laws including FMLA, ADA, EEO, OSHA, and related regulations
* Demonstrated ability to manage sensitive situations with discretion, empathy, and consistency
* Strong organizational skills with the ability to prioritize competing demands
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills
* Experience working in a multi location environment preferred


Other Requirements
  
* Must pass pre employment background and drug screening
* Occasional travel up to 20 percent may be required
* Valid driver’s license required


Position Type and Expected Hours of Work
  
This is a full\-time position with a typical work schedule of Monday – Friday, 8:00 am – 5:00 pm with overtime requirements or as directed by leadership.
  
  

Benefits
  
The Company offers a comprehensive benefits package that is designed to enhance all aspects of our employees’ well\-being. In addition to competitive pay, our benefits package includes:
  
* Health, dental, and vision plans (for team members and their families)
* Financial planning through our 401(k) program
* Additional benefits including life and disability insurance
* The Company encourages a healthy lifestyle by providing paid time off to our valued team members.


EEO Statement
  
Ontivity provides equal employment opportunities (EEO) to all employees and applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability and genetics. In addition to federal law requirements, Ontivity complies with applicable state and local laws governing nondiscrimination in employment in every location in which the company has facilities. This policy applies to all terms and conditions of employment, including recruiting, hiring, placement, promotion, termination, layoff, recall, transfer, leaves of absence, compensation and training. Ontivity expressly prohibits any form of workplace harassment based on race, color, religion, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, genetic information disability or veteran status. Improper interference with the ability of Ontivity’s employees to perform their job duties may result in discipline up to and including discharge.

title: Senior HRIS Business Analyst – Compensation & Rewards
description:
**Who is Blueprint?**



We are a technology solutions firm headquartered in Bellevue, Washington, with a strong presence across the United States. Unified by a shared passion for solving complicated problems, our people are our greatest asset. We use technology as a tool to bridge the gap between strategy and execution, powered by the knowledge, skills, and the expertise of our teams, who all have unique perspectives and years of experience across multiple industries. We're bold, smart, agile, and fun.


**What does Blueprint do?**



Blueprint helps organizations unlock value from existing assets by leveraging cutting\-edge technology to create additional revenue streams and new lines of business. We connect strategy, business solutions, products, and services to transform and grow companies.


**Why Blueprint?**



At Blueprint, we believe in the power of possibility and are passionate about bringing it to life. Whether you join our bustling product division, our multifaceted services team or you want to grow your career in human resources, your ability to make an impact is amplified when you join one of our teams. You'll focus on solving unique business problems while gaining hands\-on experience with the world's best technology. We believe in unique perspectives and build teams of people with diverse skillsets and backgrounds. At Blueprint, you'll have the opportunity to work with multiple clients and teams, such as data science and product development, all while learning, growing, and developing new solutions. We guarantee you won't find a better place to work and thrive than at Blueprint.

  


**In This Role**



In this role, you will serve as a senior functional liaison supporting enterprise compensation solutions, including promotions, rewards, and stock programs. You will work closely with Compensation, HR, Payroll, Finance, Engineering, and Data teams to ensure compensation processes are translated into clear functional requirements and delivered accurately through enterprise HR systems. You will play a key role in requirements gathering, solution validation, user acceptance testing (UAT), and release readiness to support critical compensation cycles. This position requires strong analytical skills, stakeholder engagement, and the ability to ensure data integrity, compliance, and scalable solution delivery.

  


**Responsibilities**


* Lead requirements gathering sessions with HR, Compensation, Payroll, Finance, and technical stakeholders
* Analyze business processes and translate compensation policies into functional requirements and solution designs
* Create and maintain functional documentation, including requirements, process flows, and solution specifications
* Serve as the primary liaison between business and technical teams to ensure alignment and clarity
* Conduct impact analysis and identify system, process, and data dependencies
* Define and coordinate User Acceptance Testing (UAT), including test planning, test case development, and defect tracking
* Support release planning, validation, and production readiness activities
* Ensure data accuracy, audit readiness, and compliance with compensation policies and controls
* Identify process improvement opportunities to increase efficiency and reduce operational risk
* Provide ongoing support, troubleshooting, and documentation for compensation\-related system enhancements

  


**Required Qualifications**


* Bachelor's degree in Business, Finance, Economics, Accounting, Information Systems, or related field
* 8\+ years of Business Analyst experience, preferably supporting HR, Compensation, or enterprise business systems
* Strong experience in requirements gathering, documentation, and functional analysis
* Experience supporting enterprise HR systems such as SAP SuccessFactors, Workday, or similar platforms
* Experience managing User Acceptance Testing (UAT), including test planning, defect tracking, and release validation
* Strong stakeholder management and cross\-functional coordination skills
* Excellent analytical, problem\-solving, and communication skills
* Ability to work independently and manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment
* Strong proficiency in Microsoft Excel and other business productivity tools


**Preferred Qualifications**


* Experience supporting Compensation, Rewards, Promotions, or Performance Management programs
* Experience with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central or similar HRIS platforms
* Understanding of HR data flows, integrations, and downstream system dependencies (Payroll, Finance, Reporting)
* Experience supporting enterprise system implementations or large\-scale HR initiatives
* Familiarity with audit controls, compliance requirements, and regulated business processes
* Experience working in large enterprise environments with complex stakeholder structures
* Experience supporting release cycles, change management, and production readiness activities


**Salary Range**



At Blueprint, we strive to offer competitive pay that reflects the value of our team members. Compensation for this role is influenced by a variety of factors, including skills, education, responsibilities, experience, and geographic market. For candidates based in Washington State, the anticipated salary range is **$90,000 to $100,000 per annum**. Please note that we typically do not hire new employees at the top of the posted range. Actual starting pay will be determined based on experience, skills, and internal equity. The final salary and job title may vary depending on the selected candidate's qualifications and could fall outside the stated range.


**Equal Opportunity Employer**



Blueprint Technologies, LLC is an equal employment opportunity employer. Qualified applicants are considered without regard to race, color, age, disability, sex, gender identity or expression, orientation, veteran/military status, religion, national origin, ancestry, marital, or familial status, genetic information, citizenship, or any other status protected by law.



If you need assistance or a reasonable accommodation to complete the application process, please reach out to: recruiting@bpcs.com



Blueprint believe in the importance of a healthy and happy team, which is why our comprehensive benefits package includes:


* Medical, dental, and vision coverage
* Flexible Spending Account
* 401k program
* Competitive PTO offerings
* Parental Leave
* Opportunities for professional growth and development


**Location:** Remote/ Redmond, WA

title: HR Generalist
description:
**The Opportunity:**  

Glumac, a Tetra Tech company, is adding an HR Generalist to our People Team. You'll provide hands\-on support across HR Operations and Recruiting. This role is ideal for a proactive HR professional who enjoys balancing tactical work with opportunities to support overall people strategy, improve processes, and help shape a positive employee experience in a fast\-paced corporate setting.

**Position Summary:**  

We’re looking for an HR Generalist with at least three years of corporate HR experience to support day\-to\-day HR operations and recruiting activities. You will report to our Director of People \& Development and execute transactional HR tasks, help ensure compliance with employment law, handle confidential matters with discretion, and work both independently and as part of cross\-functional teams to meet tight deadlines.

**Essential Job Functions:**  

The following duties are considered essential to the role. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform these essential functions:  

* Support HR Operations and Recruiting with transactional and tactical tasks (onboarding, data entry, offer letters, scheduling, applicant tracking support).
* Maintain and update HR records and systems accurately and confidentially.
* Apply working knowledge of employment law to routine HR processes and escalate complex issues.
* Communicate clearly and professionally with candidates, employees, and managers (written and verbal).
* Prioritize workload to meet tight deadlines in a dynamic environment.
* Use MS Outlook and MS Teams daily to coordinate and collaborate.
* Assist with basic HR reporting and ad hoc analytics to support decision\-making.
* Manage sensitive employee issues with strict confidentiality and good judgment.
* Resolve routine employee questions and conflicts; escalate when necessary.
**Required Qualifications:**  

* Minimum 3 years of HR experience in a corporate environment.
* Working knowledge of employment law and HR compliance basics.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills with strong attention to detail.
* Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and conflict\-resolution skills.
* Demonstrated ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced setting.
* Strong organizational skills and ability to prioritize multiple tasks and meet deadlines.
* Proven ability to handle confidential information professionally.
* Proficient with MS Outlook and MS Teams.
**Preferred Qualifications:**  

* Experience with ADP and Oracle
* Experience with Taleo or other applicant tracking systems.
* Experience with Asana is a plus!
**Work Environment and Location:**  

This position offers a hybrid work environment, with a requirement of two in\-office days per week in either our Portland, OR, or Seattle, WA office. All candidates must be located in and eligible to work in the U.S.

**Compensation:**  

Salary Range: $70,000 \- $90,000 (depending on experience)  

The base salary range represents the low and high end of the compensation scale for this role. Actual salaries will be determined based on location, experience, responsibilities, and qualifications. This salary range is just one component of Glumac’s total compensation package, which includes benefits, professional development opportunities, and additional incentives.

  

**Why Glumac, a Tetra Tech Company:**  

Glumac has been a leader in the sustainable building design industry for over 50 years. In 2017 we joined Tetra Tech, enabling us to combine our expertise with the reach and resources of a prestigious global organization.


At Tetra Tech, we are Leading with Science to solve the world’s most complex challenges. Our industry\-leading experts in engineering and consulting are committed to driving positive change in communities around the world. For nearly 60 years, we have been at the forefront of innovation and sustainability. Today we stand as a market leader, offering cutting\-edge solutions in water, environment, energy, and international development. Our work has improved more than 625 million lives around the world.

**Your Impact:**  

Join Glumac, a Tetra Tech company, to make a real difference. Our work leverages cutting\-edge technologies, advanced analytics, and the expertise of world\-class scientists and engineers to create meaningful change around the world. Discover your full potential – join us to advance your career while leaving a lasting legacy.

**Life at Glumac, a Tetra Tech Company:**  

The perks of working at Glumac, a Tetra Tech company, include:  

* Comprehensive and market\-competitive benefits.
* Merit\-based financial rewards.
* Flexibility and company\-wide commitment to work/life balance.
* Collaborative team atmosphere that values the contributions of all employees.
* Learning and development opportunities for ongoing professional growth.
**About Tetra Tech:**

title: HR Generalist
description:
**The Opportunity:**  

Glumac, a Tetra Tech company, is adding an HR Generalist to our People Team. You'll provide hands\-on support across HR Operations and Recruiting. This role is ideal for a proactive HR professional who enjoys balancing tactical work with opportunities to support overall people strategy, improve processes, and help shape a positive employee experience in a fast\-paced corporate setting.

**Position Summary:**  

We’re looking for an HR Generalist with at least three years of corporate HR experience to support day\-to\-day HR operations and recruiting activities. You will report to our Director of People \& Development and execute transactional HR tasks, help ensure compliance with employment law, handle confidential matters with discretion, and work both independently and as part of cross\-functional teams to meet tight deadlines.

**Essential Job Functions:**  

The following duties are considered essential to the role. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform these essential functions:  

* Support HR Operations and Recruiting with transactional and tactical tasks (onboarding, data entry, offer letters, scheduling, applicant tracking support).
* Maintain and update HR records and systems accurately and confidentially.
* Apply working knowledge of employment law to routine HR processes and escalate complex issues.
* Communicate clearly and professionally with candidates, employees, and managers (written and verbal).
* Prioritize workload to meet tight deadlines in a dynamic environment.
* Use MS Outlook and MS Teams daily to coordinate and collaborate.
* Assist with basic HR reporting and ad hoc analytics to support decision\-making.
* Manage sensitive employee issues with strict confidentiality and good judgment.
* Resolve routine employee questions and conflicts; escalate when necessary.
**Required Qualifications:**  

* Minimum 3 years of HR experience in a corporate environment.
* Working knowledge of employment law and HR compliance basics.
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills with strong attention to detail.
* Strong analytical, problem\-solving, and conflict\-resolution skills.
* Demonstrated ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast\-paced setting.
* Strong organizational skills and ability to prioritize multiple tasks and meet deadlines.

**Work Environment and Location:**

title: HR Manager - North Americas
description:
**COME JOIN OUR TEAM!**

We are a growing company that invests in our people, our products, and our technology. We offer real opportunities for career growth. Our salaries are competitive with the market, and we have a great benefits platform that includes a flexi holiday scheme, company sick pay, salary sacrifice pension, employee discount scheme, life assurance and a company bonus scheme together with a great positive culture!


**WHO WE ARE:**

Pulsar Measurement is part of the TASi Measurement group and is a worldwide leader in ultrasonic instrumentation for wastewater, industrial, and environmental markets. Since 1986 we have developed, manufactured and marketed industrial flow and level monitoring instruments including ultrasonic level transmitters, clamp\-on ultrasonic flow meters, and open channel flow meters.



The newly restructured TASi Measurement, our parent group, has successfully grown companies who consistently deliver world\-class products and services, focusing on our global customer needs in diverse market segments. TASi Measurement’s high repeat customer rate is due to an ability to remain close to our customers reinforced by providing local service and complete engineered solutions and problem solving.



Join us on this exciting journey, as we continue to expand our business.


**JOB SUMMARY:**



The North Americas (NA) HR Manager is responsible for delivering high‑quality, compliant, people‑focused HR support across four sites in the U.S. and Canada: Kent (WA), California, Florida, and Ontario. Operating as a hands‑on, confident HR Generalist, this role provides guidance to local site leaders and employees on all aspects of the employee lifecycle, including employee relations, performance management, recruitment, benefits administration (excluding payroll), and compliance with U.S. federal, state, and Canadian provincial employment legislation.



Based in Kent, WA, the HR Manager partners closely with the Senior HR Manager and the wider Global HR Team, including sites in the UK. The role is ideal for an ambitious HR professional seeking to broaden their experience within a growing global organization.



Salary on offer $100,000 \- $110,000pa.


**PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:**


**Generalist HR support across the U.S and Canada**


* Provide day‑to‑day HR support to managers and employees at the Seattle, California, Florida, and Ontario sites.
* Ensure compliance with relevant U.S. federal, state, and Canadian employment laws, advising managers on local legislative requirements.
* Manage employee relations issues from initial consultation to resolution, escalating complex matters to the Senior HR Manager when needed.
* Support the consistent application of HR policies, procedures, and company values across all sites.


**Employee Lifecycle Management**


* Oversee recruitment processes for U.S. and Canadian positions, partnering with managers to define requirements and drive high‑quality hiring decisions.
* Coordinate onboarding and offboarding processes to ensure a smooth and compliant employee experience.
* Manage performance\-related processes, including coaching managers on goal‑setting, feedback, development, and performance improvement plans.
* Support benefits administration (excluding payroll), including enrollment, employee queries, and vendor liaison where required.


**HR Operations \& Compliance**


* Maintain accurate and compliant HR records, documentation, and systems in line with U.S. and Canadian legal requirements.
* Prepare HR reports, metrics, and insights for the Senior HR Manager and business leaders.
* Participate in audits, policy reviews, and compliance checks across sites.
* Assist with workforce planning and organizational changes, including restructures, role design, and documentation.


**Culture, Engagement \& Development**


* Champion a positive, inclusive culture that promotes employee engagement and high performance.
* Support the coordination and delivery of employee engagement initiatives, recognition programs, and DEI activities.
* Provide coaching and guidance to supervisors and managers to build people‑leadership capability.


**Global HR Collaboration**


* Work as part of the Global HR Team to support cross‑regional HR processes, policies, and initiatives.
* In time, provide HR support for UK sites as required, gaining experience in international HR practices.
* Contribute to global projects such as HR process harmonization, talent initiatives, and system improvements.


**TRAVEL:**



Travel will be required to the California, Florida, and Ontario sites. Periodic travel to the UK may be required in the future to support the Global HR activities.


**KNOWLEDGE, SKILLS \& ABILITIES:**


**Experience \& Skills**


* Strong, hands‑on HR generalist capability across ER, recruitment, performance management, and HR operations.
* Demonstrated ability to advise managers on complex people matters with minimal legal risk.
* Strong knowledge of U.S. federal and state employment legislation.
* Working knowledge of Canadian employment law (ideally Ontario).
* Experience working in a multi‑site or multi‑state organization.
* Exposure to HR within a global company or experience supporting international sites.
* Experience working with HRIS platforms or supporting HR systems improvements.


**Personal Attributes**


* Excellent communication, interpersonal, and influencing skills across all levels.
* Highly organized with strong attention to detail and the ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment.
* Ability to work independently, exercise sound judgment, and maintain confidentiality.
* Professional, positive, and self‑motivated.
* Able to work calmly under pressure and manage sensitive issues effectively.
* Strong relationship‑builder with a collaborative mindset.


**EDUCATION AND COMPETENCES:**


**Professional Competence, Education \& Training**


* 5\+ years’ experience in an HR Generalist role, supporting U.S. employment matters.
* SHRM‑CP or SHRM‑SCP (or equivalent HR certification in the U.S.).
* Microsoft Office – Excel, Word, Outlook, Sharepoint.


**TRAITS:**

Ability to uphold Pulsar Measurement’s mission and vision. Must be accountable and adaptable to changing priorities, trustworthy, loyal, helpful, friendly, courteous, kind, cheerful, thrifty, and respectful.


**COMMITMENT TO PEOPLE AND PLANET:**

TASi Measurement is committed to fostering a sustainable and socially responsible environment. We believe that our success is not only measured by financial gains but also by the positive impact we have on our employees, communities, and the world around us.


As part of our commitment to people, we strive to provide a supportive and inclusive workplace where every individual is valued, respected, and given equal opportunities to thrive. We prioritize the well\-being, safety, and personal development of our employees, recognizing that they are the engine driving our success.

title: Director, Executive Talent Management
description:
*To get the best candidate experience, please consider applying for a maximum of 3 roles within 12 months to ensure you are not duplicating efforts.*

Job Category


Employee Success
Job Details


**About Salesforce**

Salesforce is the \#1 AI CRM, where humans with agents drive customer success together. Here, ambition meets action. Tech meets trust. And innovation isn’t a buzzword — it’s a way of life. The world of work as we know it is changing and we're looking for Trailblazers who are passionate about bettering business and the world through AI, driving innovation, and keeping Salesforce's core values at the heart of it all.


Ready to level\-up your career at the company leading workforce transformation in the agentic era? You’re in the right place! Agentforce is the future of AI, and you are the future of Salesforce.


**Summary**  

The global Executive Talent \& Succession (ETS) team drives the next generation of leadership at Salesforce. We don't just manage talent; we architect the future leadership bench. We engage, empower, and retain our most senior executives (SVP\+) to ensure they are agile, innovative, and strong leaders who champion human\-agent collaboration, ready to lead the future of the Agentic enterprise. Through a seamless, integrated, and AI\-first approach—from predictive succession planning and strategic onboarding to personalized, cutting\-edge development journeys—we create and sustain a strong executive leadership bench that provides a competitive advantage and keeps us a market leader.  

  



**Description**  

The Director, Executive Talent Management role builds the future leadership bench, designing and implementing executive talent management strategies and programs for our SVP\+ executives. **This role requires a visionary leader who can both define the future of executive talent strategy and operationalize it at enterprise scale.** This role ensures we are identifying, building, and preparing our current and next generation of leaders to lead Salesforce today and into the future. With a primary focus on succession planning, leader pipeline development, retention/engagement, and executive talent initiatives, this role collaborates closely with senior leaders in Employee Success and the business to develop a strong, diverse leadership bench across the enterprise.  

  



**Responsibilities:**

* **Succession Planning** \- Develop and deliver a comprehensive succession planning strategy, identifying critical leadership roles and criteria for key successors. Define measures of success and ongoing governance. Partner with senior Employee Success leaders to drive ongoing accountability for the program. Work with the data analytics team to analyze successor information and enhance the critical role dashboard. **Translate vision into scalable processes that can be executed consistently across the enterprise.** Provide support in preparing for quarterly board meetings.
* **Leadership Pipeline** \- Create and influence processes to identify, develop, and place high potential leaders for future roles within Salesforce. **Build innovative frameworks that work at scale while maintaining quality and impact.** Establish guidance and processes to enable this at scale. Partner with internal and external stakeholders to enhance successor profiles, and create greater objective assessment of readiness.
* **Executive Talent Management Strategy** \- Design and implement an AI\-led end\-to\-end executive talent management strategy roadmap, developing a framework for how to retain, engage, and move talent. **Balance big\-picture thinking with practical execution, ensuring strategies are both forward\-thinking and operationally sound.** Use data to identify success and gap areas, and identify critical business outcomes to ensure our strategy drives the highest impact. Lead implementation of a comprehensive plan focused on SVP\+ retention and engagement.
* **Talent Identification** \- Identify future\-ready skills to lead the agentic enterprise, capabilities, and experiences for key executive roles and successor readiness. Build a scalable, data\-driven assessment strategy and approach to identify key strengths and development opportunities for critical executive talent and successors.
* **Collaborate and Influence** \- Build deep partnerships with critical ES teams, including ES Leadership and Business Partners, the Office of Equality, Enterprise Learning, Executive Recruiting, Executive Onboarding, and Compensation.

**Requirements:**

* Proven track record as a subject matter expert in talent management and succession planning
* Track record of implementing AI\-driven solutions and applying systems thinking to scale talent initiatives across complex organizations
* Graduate degree or certificate in Industrial/Organizational Psychology, Leadership/Organization Development (OD), Human Resources or Business Administration, or equivalent experience
* 10\+ years of progressive Leadership/TM/OD/OE experience within large organizations or equally complex environments
* Extensive experience delivering leadership and talent programs, including succession and pipeline development practices
* Proven ability to be both visionary and operationally excellent—someone who can define innovative talent strategies and execute them at enterprise scale
* Track record of translating bold ideas into scalable processes, systems, and programs that deliver measurable results
* Deep knowledge of leadership and talent management best practices
* Strong analytical skills and a proficiency in building data\-driven talent strategies
* Track record of driving positive organizational change in a fast\-paced environment
* Strong communication, influencing, and stakeholder management skills
* Excellent written, presentation, and interpersonal communication skills

**Travel:**  

Travel 5\-10\-%


Unleash Your Potential


When you join Salesforce, you’ll be limitless in all areas of your life. Our benefits and resources support you to find balance and *be your best* , and our AI agents accelerate your impact so you can *do your best* . Together, we’ll bring the power of Agentforce to organizations of all sizes and deliver amazing experiences that customers love. Apply today to not only shape the future — but to redefine what’s possible — for yourself, for AI, and the world.


Accommodations


If you require assistance due to a disability applying for open positions please submit a request via this Accommodations Request Form .


Posting Statement


Salesforce is an equal opportunity employer and maintains a policy of non\-discrimination with all employees and applicants for employment. What does that mean exactly? It means that at Salesforce, we believe in equality for all. And we believe we can lead the path to equality in part by creating a workplace that’s inclusive, and free from discrimination. Know your rights: workplace discrimination is illegal. Any employee or potential employee will be assessed on the basis of merit, competence and qualifications – without regard to race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender expression or identity, transgender status, age, disability, veteran or marital status, political viewpoint, or other classifications protected by law. This policy applies to current and prospective employees, no matter where they are in their Salesforce employment journey. It also applies to recruiting, hiring, job assignment, compensation, promotion, benefits, training, assessment of job performance, discipline, termination, and everything in between. Recruiting, hiring, and promotion decisions at Salesforce are fair and based on merit. The same goes for compensation, benefits, promotions, transfers, reduction in workforce, recall, training, and education.


In the United States, compensation offered will be determined by factors such as location, job level, job\-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Certain roles may be eligible for incentive compensation, equity, and benefits. Salesforce offers a variety of benefits to help you live well including: time off programs, medical, dental, vision, mental health support, paid parental leave, life and disability insurance, 401(k), and an employee stock purchasing program. More details about company benefits can be found at the following link: https://www.salesforcebenefits.com.
At Salesforce, we believe in equitable compensation practices that reflect the dynamic nature of labor markets across various regions.\&\#xa;\&\#xa;The typical base salary range for this position is $164,000 \- $261,500 annually. \&\#xa;\&\#xa;The range represents base salary only, and does not include company bonus, incentive for sales roles, equity or benefits, as applicable.

title: AI Trainer
description:
**Get to know Okta**  

  

Okta is The World’s Identity Company. We free everyone to safely use any technology, anywhere, on any device or app. Our flexible and neutral products, Okta Platform and Auth0 Platform, provide secure access, authentication, and automation, placing identity at the core of business security and growth.  

  

At Okta, we celebrate a variety of perspectives and experiences. We are not looking for someone who checks every single box \- we’re looking for lifelong learners and people who can make us better with their unique experiences.  

  

Join our team! We’re building a world where Identity belongs to you.

**The Team**



You'll be joining our Okta Customer Acceleration organization, a high\-impact team focused on defining and optimizing the self\-service journey for our customers across Knowledge, Online Support, and Communities. Our mission is to transform the customer experience by enabling efficient, accurate, and engaging self\-service solutions.

  


**The Opportunity**



We're looking for an experienced **AI Trainer and Optimization Analyst** to take full ownership of our customer support agentic experiences. This is a critical specialist role that offers the chance to directly influence millions of customer interactions annually.



You will act as the **primary expert** on the AI agent's performance, applying a strategic mindset to continuously analyze conversational data and leverage analytics to enhance the bot's ability. You will be responsible for:


* Implementing **prompt engineering** best practices.
* Ensuring the agent provides accurate, efficient, and on\-brand support.
* Managing the bot's tone and ethical guardrails to prevent hallucination and incorrect assumptions.
* "Thinking bigger" to bring new, strategic ideas for leveraging the agentic experience for maximum customer impact.

  


**What you’ll be doing**



You will be the dedicated owner of the AI agent's conversational experience:


* **Own Conversational Quality \& Tone:** Act as the primary owner for the AI agent's output, managing the content, tone, and ethical guardrails to ensure responses are accurate, on\-brand, and avoid inappropriate or incorrect assumptions and "hallucination."
* **Prompt Engineering \& Training:** Develop, test, and deploy advanced prompt engineering techniques, training materials, and conversational flows to enhance the AI agent's accuracy, intent recognition, and overall effectiveness.
* **Performance Analysis \& Optimization:** Proactively monitor and analyze AI agent performance metrics, including deflection rates, resolution rates, and customer satisfaction (CSAT) scores, translating data into actionable training and optimization plans.
* **Gap Identification \& Remediation:** Review and categorize high\-volume AI conversations to identify new customer intents, common misclassifications, and critical content gaps in the knowledge base that require remediation.
* **Strategic Collaboration:** Work closely with the Content, Knowledge, and Customer Experience teams to understand common customer issues and translate those insights into strategic AI training plans, focusing on high\-ticket volume areas.
* **Continuous Improvement:** Maintain and update conversation scripts to reflect product and policy changes, and continuously identify and report on agent bugs, system gaps, and areas for potential escalation.
* **Thought Leadership:** Stay current with the latest trends and technologies in conversational AI, machine learning, and prompt engineering, bringing new ideas and strategic proposals to the table.

  


**What you’ll bring to the role**



We are looking for a specialist who can operate with strong independence and act as a domain expert.


* **Deep Conversational AI Experience:** Proven experience in a role involving AI training, prompt engineering, Natural Language Processing (NLP), or conversational design, specifically for customer support or enterprise service systems.
* **Analytical Rigor:** Strong analytical skills with a proven ability to interpret large sets of conversational data and performance metrics to draw meaningful, actionable insights for AI training and system tuning.
* **Platform Proficiency:** Familiarity with major AI conversational platforms (e.g., Dialogflow, IBM Watson, Rasa, or similar enterprise\-level tools).
* **Technical Communication:** Excellent written communication skills, with a keen eye for detail and the ability to write clear, concise, and helpful responses that maintain a professional tone.
* **Ecosystem Understanding:** Ability to understand the broader online customer support ecosystem, ensuring an integrated and seamless omnichannel experience when handing off between the bot and human agents.
* **Strategic Mindset:** A proactive, problem\-solving approach with the ability to "think bigger" and bring new, strategic ideas for leveraging the agentic experience for maximum customer impact.

title: Senior Vice President - Chief Human Resources Officer, MultiCare
description:
**THE OPPORTUNITY**


MultiCare, a dynamic integrated healthcare delivery system in the Pacific Northwest, recognizes its greatest asset is its people. The organization also recognizes caring for others as a calling and strongly believes in the importance of empowering individuals to grow, develop unique talents, and contribute their best to something bigger than themselves, which is rooted in their deep mission *of partnering for healing and a healthy future*.


Through this lens, MultiCare is seeking a purpose\-driven **Senior Vice President \- Chief Human Potential Officer** **(CHRO equivalent)** to lead human capital strategies and operations focused on developing the organization’s workforce by fostering a thriving, creative, and high\-performing culture. This role is pivotal in driving the organization forward, reinforcing its values\-driven culture and deep commitment to patient care.  

With more than 23,000 team members and a network of 13 hospitals, MultiCare ($7B net revenues) has grown from a Tacoma\-centric, hospital\-based organization into the largest, not\-for\-profit, community\-based, and locally governed health system in the state of Washington. MultiCare is on a fast growth trajectory with the goal of serving one third of the population in Washington, Oregon, Montana, Idaho, and Alaska by 2026 through a combination of direct service provision or in partnership with others. To fulfill that goal, MultiCare has been embarking on market expansion, improving access to care and services through a variety of vehicles, a performance excellence program and population health strategy.  

MultiCare understands the importance of aligning human capital strategies with overall business imperatives to ensure people are positioned for success to achieve the organization’s mission. This is an exceptional opportunity for a creative, mission\-oriented, and experienced HR executive to lead this function within a nationally recognized healthcare system with a distinguished history of community purpose. The ideal candidate will possess comprehensive functional HR experience, coupled with a strong disposition toward unlocking and nurturing the unique talents and growth of every employee.  

MultiCare has been a long\-time community pillar by bringing the best in clinical research and patient outcomes anchored by a people\-centric culture that encourages creativity, collaboration, and continuous learning, where people feel empowered and inspired to contribute.  

Reporting to the President, the Chief Human Potential Officer will set the vision for a strategic, transparent, and customer\-service\-oriented Human Resources function. S/he will work closely with the senior leadership team to achieve the next chapter of excellence at MultiCare by executing a strategic vision focused on optimizing access to care, workforce development and stability, and a continued commitment to health equity across all communities served. **CANDIDATE QUALIFICATIONS** **Education/Certification*** Master’s degree in business, HR, organizational design/development, or related discipline required.

  

  

**Experience and Professional Qualifications*** Ten (10\) years of experience in a senior, professional\-level position in Human Resources or Health Care Industry role.
* Seven (7\) years of progressively responsible management experience.
* Demonstrated strong and effective consulting and leadership coaching skills.
* Experience building a high\-functioning HR team.
* Experience moving the HR team forward as a strategic function; partnering in a high\-growth organization with a customer service mindset.
* In\-depth knowledge of the healthcare workforce shortages and strategies in workforce management (recruitment, selection, development, and retention) in both union and non\-union environments.
* Innovative and proven critical thinking, decision\-making, and relationship skills.
* Highly relational, adaptable, and an outstanding, open communicator.
* Leads by example; demonstrates outstanding character and personifies the ethics and values of MultiCare both within and outside the organization.
* Skilled in contract negotiations and management; excellent problem solver and influencer.

 *\*\*\*\*\*\**

*Korn Ferry shall provide equal employment opportunity to all qualified candidates, and will refer candidates without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, age, disability, veteran status or any other legally protected basis.*

title: Benefits, Leaves & HRIS Manager
description:
Benefits, Leaves \& HRIS Manager  


Location: Open (On\-site at any of our local Offices)  


Salary Range: 80,000 – 90,000 annually  


Serengeti Care is a growing, multi\-state home care organization dedicated to providing compassionate, high\-quality care to our clients and supportive, people\-centered experiences for our employees. We are seeking an experienced Benefits, Leaves \& HRIS Manager to lead our benefits, leave of absence, workers’ compensation, and HRIS functions.  


About the Role  


The Benefits, Leaves \& HRIS Manager is responsible for the strategy, administration, and continuous improvement of Serengeti Care’s total rewards programs and HR systems. This role oversees group benefits (medical, dental, vision, life, disability, retirement), leave of absence programs (FMLA, state leaves, ADA), workers’ compensation, and HRIS configuration and reporting. You will partner closely with HR, Payroll, Finance, Operations, and external vendors to ensure our programs are competitive, compliant, and easy for employees and managers to understand and use.  


Key Responsibilities  


Lead design, implementation, renewal, and ongoing management of all employee benefits programs.  


Oversee day\-to\-day benefits administration, including enrollments, changes, terminations, COBRA, and vendor file feeds.  


Administer and track leave of absence programs (FMLA, state leave programs, ADA, personal/medical leaves), including communication and return\-to\-work coordination.  


Coordinate workers’ compensation claims and partner with carriers, managers, and employees throughout the process.  


Ensure compliance with ERISA, COBRA, HIPAA, ACA, FMLA, ADA, and applicable federal and state regulations.  


Serve as primary HRIS owner for HR, managing configuration, workflows, security, and data integrity.  


Develop and run HR and benefits reports (headcount, turnover, leave utilization, enrollment, costs) to support leadership decisions.  


Create and maintain communication materials, guides, and trainings for benefits, leaves, and HRIS self\-service.  


Provide timely, accurate, and empathetic support to employees and leaders regarding benefits, leaves of absence, workers’ comp, and HRIS access.  


Lead or support cross\-functional projects related to benefits, HRIS, and process improvement.  


Qualifications  


Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field required.  


5–10 years of progressive experience in benefits administration and/or leave management, preferably in a multi\-state environment.  


Strong knowledge of employee benefits, leave laws, workers’ compensation, and related regulatory requirements.  


Hands\-on experience with HRIS and benefits administration systems, including configuration and reporting.  


Excellent verbal and written communication skills; able to explain complex information in a clear, supportive way.  


Strong analytical, organizational, and time management skills; comfortable managing multiple priorities and deadlines.  


High level of integrity, discretion, and professional judgment when handling sensitive information.  


HR certifications such as SHRM\-CP, SHRM\-SCP, CEBS, or PMI are preferred but not required.  


Schedule \& Work Environment  


This is a full\-time, exempt position based at our corporate office in Renton, WA. Core office hours are Monday–Friday, with occasional overtime during peak periods such as open enrollment, renewals, or system changes.  


Serengeti Care Partners is an Equal Opportunity Employer and complies with ADA regulations.  


\#HP  


About Serengeti Care  
Bringing quality care to your door! Serengeti Care is tailored to each client’s unique needs and is dedicated to providing affordable, high\-quality in\-home care. Our approach fosters trust, preserves dignity, and delivers unparalleled personalized care.

title: Union Exempt - Human Resources Specialist @ Learning Services Center
description:
**HUMAN RESOURCES SPECIALIST**

**Union Exempt**

**Leaves \- Substitutes \- Coaches**

 **TERMS OF EMPLOYMENT**


Hours: 8\.0 hrs/day; 260 days/year


Pay: $36\.63 \- $40\.70/hr


Supervisor: Human Resources Director

 **QUALIFICATIONS**

* High School Diploma or equivalent, AA degree or equivalent, or comparable work experience.
* Demonstrated five (5\) years of successful Employee Human Resources experience preferred.
* Must have excellent communication, interpersonal, planning/organization, critical thinking, and problem\-solving skills.
* Must have a proven record of observing strict confidentiality.
* Must have a proven record of punctuality, dependability, and consistent attendance.
* Must have advanced computer and employee records management skills.
* Understanding of and experience in properly applying federal, state, and district rules, regulations, procedures, and collective bargaining agreements relating to all aspects of human resources.
* Ability to interpret and apply labor contract provisions relating to employee rights and responsibilities.
* Demonstrated proficiency in verbal and written skills.
* Demonstrated ability to work independently and exercise sound judgment to organize, set priorities, coordinate activities, and complete tasks in a timely manner.
* Demonstrated ability to work within designated bounds of authority.
* Ability to work accurately and efficiently under pressure with frequent interruptions, while maintaining a positive and professional demeanor.
* Ability to establish and maintain effective, positive working relationships with staff and the public.
* Ability to work a flexible schedule at times to meet deadlines and schedules of employees and special projects.
* Strong analytical and time management skills.

 **ESSENTIAL JOB DUTIES**

* Organize, set priorities, and meet inflexible deadlines under considerable pressure.
* Maintain a positive, productive, and cooperative relationship with staff.
* Develop effective user\-friendly information that is available to all employees to ensure clarity with regard to schedules, processes, resolution of concerns, and response to questions.
* Independently perform a variety of complex and detailed tasks quickly with a high degree of accuracy.
* Protect confidential information consistent with district ethical guidelines and state/federal laws.
* Interact professionally and respectfully with staff and the public in all forms of communication, verbal and written.
* Provide exemplary customer service.
* Demonstrate confidence and professionalism when working with employees and the public.
* Remain flexible when handling numerous interruptions.
* Oversee and manage employee leaves of absence; coordinate the district attendance management program in cooperation with district administrators and supervisors.
* Oversee the coordination of coaching staff, including job postings, hiring, issuing contracts, submitting pay contracts to payroll, separations, and tracking all relevant information.
* Exercise judgment to appropriately refer matters to administrators as needed.
* Maintain employee files, including personnel, medical, electronic, investigative, etc.
* Compile data for use in collective bargaining, including comparisons of wages, benefits, and contract language.
* Maintain a strong working knowledge of collective bargaining agreements and act as a resource for district employees, supervisors, and administrators.
* Prepare confidential letters, memos, various forms, and other documents.
* Perform routine office tasks that include filing, mailings, and reception.
* Administer applicant testing.
* Develop and process job postings and the required documentation in recruitment software.
* Collect, verify, and organize all necessary paperwork for initial applicants being processed for employment.
* Research, compile, and prepare reports.
* Issue and track NORA (Notice of Reasonable Assurance) notifications for substitutes.
* Track substitute hours for all substitutes.
* Process requests for Emergency Substitute Certification (EST) for classified staff and substitutes.
* Assist building and department administrators in coordinating substitute staff assignments.
* Coordinate student teachers/interns and non\-employees onsite for clearance and system access.
* Record and monitor records for compliance with federal, state statutes and guidelines, district policy, and collective bargaining agreements.
* Provide support and assistance for users of the Absence Management software system for attendance and substitute assignments.
* Assist with collective bargaining and with the interpretation of the collective bargaining agreements as requested.
* Work cooperatively with the Administration Office staff to facilitate completion of work within timelines.
* Process purchase orders.
* Coordinate and develop orientation and annual training programs.
* Process verifications of experience for substitutes and coaches for salary placement.
* Provide back\-up to other HR specialists and other positions in the office as directed.
* Assist with the development, interpretation, and administration of human resources policy and procedures.
* Maintain archived files in accordance with retention requirements.
* Perform other job\-related duties as required.
