title: HR Consultant
description:
**Who we are**



Over 600 employees strong, Asure sees Human Capital Management (HCM) through the lens of entrepreneurs and executives with an owner’s mentality. We help businesses develop their “Human Capital” to get to the next level, stay compliant, and allocate their time, money and technology toward growth. Our HCM platform empowers more than 100,000 clients to build great teams and better manage their people while staying compliant in an ever\-changing HR legislative landscape. Asure’s HCM offering includes Payroll \& Tax, HR, and Time \& Attendance software and HR Services ranging from online compliance tools to a fully outsourced HR department.

 ***This is a remote position based in Seattle, WA. The ideal candidate would be expected to regularly travel to client sites.***

 **How you will contribute**



Our HR Consultant is responsible for maintaining the overall relationship with clients. Continuously resells Asure to the client by demonstrating Asure’s value and building client relationships.

  


* Manages the HR function for clients maintaining the parameters of the contract.
* Provides strategic guidance to clients via virtual meetings and occasionally visits in person to ensure client satisfaction and retention. Establishes and drives human resource priorities; ensures key HR issues are addressed.
* Interfaces with clients to understand their overall objectives and requirements. For each client, prepares and maintains a plan to identify short\- and long\-term goals and how to meet them within Asure’s scope of service. Reports progress on these goals to the client on a weekly basis.
* Identifies compliance requirements affecting Human Resource functions and, working with the HR Consulting team, ensures policies, procedures, and reporting systems are in place.
* Serves as primary point of contact for contract renewal and fulfillment issues.
* Creates and conducts training on HR topics for clients.
* Proactively promotes Asure services and offers appropriate additional services as needed such as Asure payroll, benefits and payroll administration and COBRA.
* Attends client management meetings virtually on a consistent basis.
* Organizes and performs client on\-site HR functions (e.g., training, investigations, and new employee orientations) and addresses immediate needs while on\-site (client may chose virtual).
* Establishes solid relationships with key contacts and managers at each client and proactively create a strategic HR plan.
* Oversee recruitment efforts such as ad creation, placement, creating compliant questions and working with outside recruitment agencies.
* Develops and maintains subject matter expertise in functional human resources topics.
* Contributes proactively to Asure process improvement.
* Communicates and works effectively with the HR Consulting team to keep everyone abreast of clients’ HR and other internal issues. Communicates and works effectively with other ASURE staff members as needed.
* Effectively and completely records and tracks documents, client activities, contacts, and deadlines such that other ASURE employees can locate and comprehend client incidents and issues. Ensures documentation serves two requirements: 1\) services clients, and 2\) accurately documents ASURE involvement.
* Acts as the main point of contact for client employee relations issues ensuring compliance with local, state and federal laws.
* Utilize HRIS system for HR functions as directed by the client.
* Performs other duties as assigned to support overall Asure client needs.

  


**Qualifications**


* Bachelor’s Degree (B.A.) from four\-year college or university, and five or more years of human resources experience and/or training; or equivalent combination of education and experience.
* Professional Human Resource Certification (PHR or SPHR) or other HR certification required.
* Excellent and detailed knowledge of local, state, and federal employment law and accepted human resources practices. Ability to research human resources issues to answer client questions.
* Ability to define problems, collect data, establish facts, and draw valid conclusions. Ability to interpret and apply a wide variety of technical information and to deal with multiple abstract and concrete variables.
* Ability to take a client relationship approach to client interactions and to focus on maintaining and expanding client involvement within Asure’s scope of service.
* Ability to respond professionally to a wide variety of human resource inquires and situations that may arise in the client setting while quickly adapting to a variety of client styles and cultures.
* Initiative, good judgment, and a high standard of professional business ethics. Professional manner required in appearance, speech, and presentation of self and developed materials.
* Ability to maintain highly confidential information received from clients and Asure.
* Excellent client management skills. Ability to balance the management of client expectations with the provision of excellent and timely customer service.
* Excellent communication skills (written, oral, and presentation) with the ability to adapt communications to instruct and/or persuade.
* Ability to work as part of a team, by contributing and accepting assistance when needed.
* Ability to effectively document client interactions.
* Ability to handle frequent interruptions from clients and co\-workers.
* Highly organized, exercising independent judgment and discretion. Ability to work independently with a minimum level of supervision.
* Computer literacy, excellent MS Office Skills, SalesForce, Teams, RingCentral, salary bench marking technology and close attention to detail.
* Experience with workplace investigations, employee relations, training and creating HR policies and procedures.
* Bilingual English/Spanish written and oral strongly preferred.
* Ability to travel locally to clients for on\-site engagements and meetings.

  


**Compensation**



The base salary range for this role is $85,000\.00 \- $100,000\.00 depending upon level of experience.

  

This role offers a comprehensive benefit package.

 **Benefits**


* Medical, Dental, Vision, HSA, FSA\- All effective on day 1!
* Company paid Basic Life Insurance, A\+D, Long\-Term Disability, and Short\-Term Disability
* 401K Program with 3% safe harbor contribution
* Employee Stock Purchase Program
* Fitness Reimbursement Program
* Self\-Managed PTO

  


**Our Mission**



To provide Human Capital Management (HCM) software and services that help companies grow, while nurturing a culture of growth around us.


* Helping customers grow by getting the most from their human capital.
* Helping our employees grow personally and professionally.
* Growing relationships in our communities that inspire goodness.
* Grow shareholder value

  


**Our Vision**



Be the most trusted Human Capital Management resource to entrepreneurs everywhere.

 **Our Values**



Reflect who we are and what we stand for as a company.


* Embrace Change
* Lead with Integrity
* Own the Outcome
* Deliver Awesome
* Be a Good Human

  


*We are an equal opportunity employer.*

  

NO AGENCIES PLEASE:


*Any unsolicited resumes sent to Asure Software from a third party, such as an Agency, including unsolicited resumes sent to Asure’s mailing address, fax machine or email address, directly to employees or hiring managers will be considered Asure property. Asure will not pay a fee for any placement resulting from the receipt of an unsolicited resume.*

title: Human Resources - Specialist (part-time)
description:
The Puyallup Tribe of Indians is committed to protecting and exercising the inherent inalienable sovereign rights of the Tribe and Individuals in the interest of the Medicine Creek Treat Territories, as stewards to ensure preservation of our cultural and environmental integrity for common good and prosperity for all.
  
  

**Closes:** March 19, 2026
  
**Job Title:** HR Specialist Department: Human Resources
  
**Reports To:** Executive Director or Designee Hours: Part\-Time 20 Hrs/Week
  
**Salary:** Based on Salary Matrix \+ Benefits Requisition: 866
  
  

**Position Summary:**  

This position performs moderately to complex duties in specialized areas of HR. Position may be assigned to recruitment, benefits, training, HRIS, employee relations or a combination of areas dependent on the department’s needs. Work assignments will vary, require input into the development of processes and procedures, assist with the collection of facts and data and coordinate activities for the department staff.
  
  

Essential Duties and Responsibilities
  
This position will be assigned duties and responsibilities operating within the functional areas of Human Resources to include Recruitment, Training, Benefits, HRIS, and Employee Relations which support the overall operation of the department. As department workloads change, this position will be expected to provide support where needed.
  
General
  
1\. Provides support to the HR department by assisting with day\-to\-day administrative duties such as answering phones, customer service, data entry, routing documents and providing back up support to the HR Assistants and department staff.
  
2\. Responsible for creating general correspondence, preparing and managing reports and data and maintaining logs and tracking worksheets.
  
3\. Assists in all areas of HR, but may be assigned tasks and projects dependent upon the workload experienced within the department.
  
4\. Performs other duties as assigned.
  
Recruitment
  
5\. 5\. Provides guidance and support to applicants throughout the hiring process, including explaining job requirements, outlining hiring procedures, and notifying applicants of selection outcomes.
  
  

6\. 6\. Offers individualized assistance to Tribal Member applicants by conducting one\-on\-one intake appointments and providing ongoing support throughout the hiring process.
  
  

7\. Responsible for managing hiring process from beginning to end, including job posting, applicant review process, interview and selection process, preparing hiring documents, and onboarding process.
  
8\. Processes Emergency Hire requests, prepares a list of qualified candidates for the hiring manager and Assistant Executive Director’s review.
  
9\. Assists the Assistant Executive Director with researching, writing, reviewing, and editing job descriptions as needed, including compensation market research.
  
10\. Assists with transfer and promotion requests, determines if it meets policy requirements. Prepares checklist, memo and routes for signature.
  
11\. Responsible for managing the Hiring Tracking Worksheet, and ensures that hiring timelines are met
  
12\. Develops recruiting plans to attract qualified applicants and fills specific job openings and works with external recruiters, as needed, to identify potential candidates, and researches new places to advertise open positions.
  
13\. Works directly with WFD department to assist Tribal Members with obtaining employment. Invites WFD Director to observe Tribal Member interviews.
  
14\. Prepares a weekly summary of job openings for the Tribal Communications department.
  
15\. Handles the Law Enforcement (LE) recruitment process. Schedules and conducts LE candidate physical and written testing. Works with LE and outside entities to arrange LE background checks and psychological, polygraph and medical testing.
  
16\. Arranges travel and lodging for out\-of\-town candidates as necessary. Assists new employees with relocation assistance when applicable.
  
17\. Responsible for ensuring compliance with the Hiring Preference Policy. Clearly communicates policy requirements during interviews and assists hiring managers and panels in reviewing and selecting applicants in alignment with the policy. Requires a strong understanding of the policy and the ability to balance departmental needs with requirements. Assists the HR Team with the preparation of quarterly hiring/ethnicity reports to Tribal Council.
  
  

Benefits
  
18\. Provides FML paperwork to employees, provides timely service of mandatory notices as required by FMLA; tracks FML hours for full time and intermittent FML, provides weekly report to Administrative Manager of employees on FML. Processes leave donation requests as needed, and tracks donated hours. Conducts employee leave studies as needed.
  
19\. Assists employees with retirement plan questions, including distribution, hardship and loan inquiries. Directs employees to retirement platform website to request distributions and loans. Notifies payroll of new loans and repayment plans. Send quarterly 401k eligibility notices, and annual catch up notices. Distributes disclosure notices and participant statements. Assists with Retirement Plan audit by gathering documents and researching questions and findings posed by auditors. Assists with preparation of the annual retirement plan census. Reviews quarterly report from the plan recordkeeper to ensure compliance with retirement loan program.
  
20\. Conducts new hire orientation regarding employee benefit enrollment. Processes benefit enrollments, and sends General Notices. On a monthly basis, reconciles benefit invoices by comparing with a system benefit election report.
  
  

21\. Acts as a liaison between employee and benefit provider for coverage questions or issues, and communicates information back to the employee ensuring his/her understanding of the situation. Escalates severe situations with the benefit administrators and acts as the point of contact on behalf of the employee.
  
22\. Conducts exit interviews, collects tribal property from employee upon separation, notifies benefit provider of terminations for COBRA notices to be sent.
  
23\. Assists with benefits open enrollment including, communication to employees, review employee benefit change requests, coordinating annual Benefits Fair. Prepares new plan year benefit rate sheets for Accounting.
  
24\. Uploads bi\-weekly payroll information to the ACA tracking database and enters employee status changes to database. Monitors benefit eligibility status for non\-full time employees. Assist with the annual filing of ACA forms.
  
25\. Processes and tracks employee’s workers’ compensation claims, and oversees return to work and light duty accommodations.
  
26\. Prepares “snooze\-lose” notices for employees with excess vacation hours.
  
27\. Schedules the following events; annual flu shot clinic, bi\-annual participant meetings with 401k investment advisors.
  
HRIS
  
28\. Responsible for data entry into the HRIS system, including personal data, employment/compensation data, benefit information, training certificates, performance and discipline data, etc.
  
29\. Generates various reports from the HRIS database as requested by HR staff. Develops custom reports via the system report builder.
  
30\. Acts as a lead to troubleshoot issues, manage employee questions, projects involving HRIS, and researches system updates. Keeps the HR Director apprised of system updates that will require change to current processes.
  
31\. Acts as the point of contact for self\-service questions/issues from Administration staff, assists with on\-going training for employee self\-service portal.
  
32\. Creates new processes to assist HR in daily duties, and creates user guides for processes.
  
33\. Responsible for performing research if a new HRIS system or program is needed.
  
34\. Trains new HR staff on HRIS system.
  
35\. Submits and tracks tickets with the system support portal as issues arise. Meets with support representatives as needed.
  
36\. Gathers data for HR staff as required for the quarterly report.
  
37\. Performs monthly audits to ensure reliable and accurate data within HRIS system.
  
38\. Responsible for entering invoices in the Microix payment database.
  
Employee Relations
  
39\. Responsible for coordinating meetings or interviews.
  
40\. May participate as a witness to meetings and take notes.
  
41\. Prepares materials for grievance hearings.
  
42\. Assists the Sr. Generalist with drafting letters and notices regarding employee relation matters.
  
43\. Provides information for unemployment inquiries.
  
44\. Performs intake for employee relation complaints.
  
45\. Assists supervisors with drafting employee relations documents.
  
46\. Works with supervisors on progressive disciplinary steps and issuing notices to employees.
  
Training
  
47\. Responsible for scheduling trainings by securing training room and registering participants.
  
48\. Assists with the development of training materials.
  
49\. Creates notices and flyers to advertise training program to tribal employees.
  
50\. Tracks attendance for mandatory trainings, including new hire orientation.
  
51\. Assists Sr. Generalist with RFPs pertaining to training.
  
52\. Performs orientation and policy training as assigned by Sr. Generalist.
  
53\. Researches potential trainings and trainers for Sr. Generalist review.
  
54\. May assist with coaching and mentoring employees to manage conflicts at the lowest level.
  
Supervisory Responsibilities
  
This position has no supervisory responsibilities. May provide training to other staff.
  
Qualifications and Requirements
  
To perform this job successfully, an individual must be able to perform each essential duty satisfactorily. The requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
  
  

Education and/or Experience
  
AA degree from an accredited technical school, community college or university and two years of related work experience in the HR or related field; or High school diploma or general education degree (GED) and four years related work experience in Human Resources or a related field; or other satisfactory equivalent combination of related education and experience. Intermediate to Advanced experience and knowledge using word processing, spreadsheets and database (i.e. Word for Windows, Excel, Access, etc.) is also required.
  
Language Skills
  
Ability to read, analyze, and interpret common scientific and technical journals, financial reports, and legal documents. Ability to respond to common inquiries or complaints from customers, regulatory agencies, or members of the community. Ability to write reports and collect data and facts that conform to prescribed style and format. Courtesy, accuracy, confidentiality and customer service are a significant aspect of the job.
  
Mathematical Skills
  
Ability to add, subtract, multiply, and divide in all units of measure, using whole numbers, common fractions, and decimals. Ability to compute rate, ratio, and percent and to draw and interpret bar graphs.
  
Reasoning Ability
  
Ability to define problems, collect data, establish facts, and draw valid conclusions. Ability to interpret an extensive variety of technical instructions in mathematical or diagram form and deal with several abstract and concrete variables. Ability to understand how to set priorities, develop processes and procedures, make sound decisions with little supervision and manage multiple assignments, projects and tasks.
  
Licenses or Certificates
  
Must have and maintain a valid and unrestricted Washington State Driver’s License and proof of auto insurance. Driver’s License must not contain any restrictions that would otherwise prevent an employee from operating a GSA vehicle and complying with the Tribe’s Vehicle Use Policy.
  
May be required to pass a background check prior to and periodically throughout employment.
  
Must be able to work in a diverse environment with tact and diplomacy and be culturally sensitive.
  
Must be able to demonstrate superior customer service skills.
  
Must be able to maintain the strictest confidentiality at all times.
  
Other Abilities
  
Strong customer service skills.
  
Ability to have difficult conversations and remain calm in stressful situations.
  
Requires knowledge of Tribal departments and services.
  
May be assigned to work evenings and weekends for recruitment events.
  
Physical Demands
  
The physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
  
  

1\. Requires the ability to operate a personal computer, multi\-line telephone, calculator, facsimile and photocopier.
  
2\. Requires the ability to read, write, communicate, and interpret information accurately in English.
  
3\. Requires the ability to concentrate and consistently produce accurate work.
  
4\. While performing the duties of this job, the employee is frequently required to sit; use hands to finger, handle, or feel; reach with hands and arms; and talk or hear.
  
5\. The employee occasionally is required to stand and walk.
  
6\. The employee must occasionally lift and/or move up to 10 pounds.
  
7\. Specific vision abilities required by this job include close vision, color vision and ability to adjust focus.
  
Work Environment
  
The work environment characteristics described here are representative of those an employee encounters while performing the essential functions of this job. Reasonable accommodations may be made to enable individuals with disabilities to perform the essential functions.
  
  

8\. The noise level is low.
  
9\. The physical exertion is low to moderate.
  
10\. Work is performed in an office setting.
  
11\. There are frequent employee contacts and interruptions during the day.
  
12\. There is a need to meet tight deadlines and regularly stop and restart work assignments.
  
13\. Position may be stressful at times due to the sensitive or confidential issues being addressed.
  
  

The Puyallup Tribe of Indians reserves the right to revise or change job duties and responsibilities as the need arises. This position description does not constitute a written or implied contract of employment.
  
Indian Preference Employer as Required by Law
  
The undersigned has reviewed this job description and understands and acknowledges that this job description is a complete description of the duties and responsibilities of the position, and further acknowledges that they are able to perform the essential functions and physical demands as required by the position.
  
  

Native American preference applies and job placements are given on a competitive basis, using job related factors. Because of the large number of applications received and limited job positions, your application will not guarantee employment.

title: Human Resources Generalist
description:
**Position Open Date: 03/06/2026**


**Position Close Date: 03/13/2026**

  


**Interested in making a difference in your community while building a career? Join our team as an HR Generalist!**


**WHO WE ARE:**



For 60 years, Opportunity Council has worked throughout Northwest Washington to build just, equitable communities through service, investment, and collaboration. Our mission is to help people improve their lives through education, support, and direct assistance while advocating for just and equitable communities. Part of a national network of Community Action Agencies, we are dedicated to helping people help each other and themselves, building a community where everyone has the chance to thrive and contribute.


**OUR VALUES:**



At Opportunity Council, our values guide everything we do. Collaboration, respect, integrity, passion, and learning shape how we show up for each other and for our community. We’re also committed to weaving diversity, equity, and inclusion into every part of our work—from the help we offer in the community to the kind of workplace we create for our staff.



To learn more about us and our work, visit our website: www.oppco.org

  


**WORK SCHEDULE:** Monday through Friday 8 AM \- 5 PM

  


**WHAT YOU WILL DO:**



The Human Resources (HR) Generalist provides day\-to\-day operational support for Opportunity Council’s Human Resources team to provide Opportunity Council with supportive people\-focused infrastructure based upon the agency’s strategic plan and mission to create an engaged and client\-focused workforce. The HR Generalist is responsible for supporting many critical HR functions, including recruiting and onboarding; safety compliance; worker’s compensation; family \& medical leave; workplace accommodations; HR information systems, databases and self\-service systems; and regulatory reporting requirements and data requests. In addition, the HR Generalist will serve as a cross\-functional member of the HR team, providing support and backup in all areas of human resources. The HR Generalist will assist in shaping HR processes to promote a collaborative working environment and an organizational culture that supports and embraces equity and social justice for the organization’s employees and the community.

  


**ESSENTIAL FUNCTIONS AND RESPONSIBILITIES:**


**Regulatory Compliance:**


* Administers the Workers Compensation Program (L\&I), working with employees, managers and providers.
* In coordination with HR Director(s) completes HR, agency, and external requests for data and reports. Responsible for HR compliance reporting, including EEOC, OSHA, and Washington State Labor \& Industries.
* Ensures HR department and Opportunity Council are in compliance with federal, state, and local laws, regulations, directives, and other compliance requirements.
* Assists with document/file/HR Systems audits, ensuring compliance with Federal, State and local laws, regulations, directives, and other compliance requirements.


**Benefits \& Leave:**


* Reviews requests for workplace accommodations, manages interactive process, makes accommodation determinations, provides job applicant accommodations.
* Oversees FMLA and PFMLA medical leave and workplace accommodation programs. With guidance from HR Director(s), makes determinations regarding eligibility, and release to work for leave and accommodations related to medical, military, domestic violence, and parental leave. Notifies employees of eligibility and approval of leave, transfers process to leave vendor and tracks progress, manages internal and external communications.
* Provides support and backup for employee benefits program. Supports coordinating monthly benefits processing and data transfer, including new hire benefits enrollment, benefits changes, and claims. Supports annual Open Enrollment. Serves as backup contact for plan vendors and third\-party administrators. Ensures agency compliance with applicable government regulations. Provides customer service support to internal and external customers. Works with Benefits Coordinator to develop and design communications and communication materials and tools to enhance understanding of agency’s benefits.


**Recruiting \& Onboarding:**


* Provides support and backup for the recruitment program, including reviewing and editing work plans for vacant and new positions, conducting classification and compensation analysis for vacant and new positions, posting vacant and new positions on recruitment software, tracking posted positions through the recruitment process on recruitment software, assisting hiring managers with the recruitment process, setting up interviews, and corresponding with applicants as needed.
* Provides support and backup for new employee orientation and onboarding program, including facilitating all necessary paperwork, coordinating orientation and training dates, and ensuring new employees and departments have coordinated departmental and agency onboarding.
* Supports the administration of Drug Free Workplace policies and practices and Coordinates Commercial Drivers’ License (CDL) drug testing process.


**Performance Reviews:**


* Assists HR Director with Performance Management software implementation, rollout and program management. Includes communication with managers and employees on the importance of process.


**Policy \& HRMS Analytics:**


* Maintains knowledge of trends, best practices, regulatory changes, and new technologies in human resources, and employment law; applies this knowledge to educate and communicate changes in policies, practices, and resources to HR leadership and team.
* Provides assistance with updating and developing OC Personnel Policies to mitigate risk and maintain compliance.
* Administers human resources related software, working with HR information systems as required to create, run, and analyze reports.


**Training \& Development:**


* Participate in work groups and project teams focused on the strategic relationship of HR within the organization. Participate in HR initiatives and on other HR cross\-functional teams as needed.
* Assists with agency trainings as needed.
* Participates in work groups, committees and project teams focused on the strategic relationship of HR staff within the organization. Participates in HR initiatives and on other HR cross\-functional teams as needed.
* Works in collaboration with HR Leadership to communicate HR goals in alignment with agency mission to organization is clear and respectful manner.

  


**OUR IDEAL CANDIDATE WILL HAVE:**


* Bachelor’s Degree in related field or equivalent year\-for\-year experience in human resources.
* Minimum four years demonstrated experience in HR functional areas or applicable experience.
* Demonstrated experience with HR or similar systems, including report writing from relational databases.
* Must be proficient in Microsoft Office applications.
* Preferred SHRM and/or HRCI Human Resources certification.
* Must have valid Washington State driver’s license and maintain insurability through agency insurer for duration of employment.
* If hiring for a bilingual position. Must be able to speak, read, and write with proficiency and fluency in the required language.

  



We place a strong value in hiring individuals with lived experiences of, poverty and/or homelessness or who have previously participated in Opportunity Council programs or other social services.

  


**OUR BENEFITS PACKAGE:**



We offer a comprehensive benefits package designed to support your well\-being, growth, and work\-life balance.


* Paid time off earned on an accrual basis (vacation, sick, personal, holidays)
* Paid time off for wellness needs
* Employer\-paid holidays
* Employer\-paid HDHP and low\-cost traditional health care options
* HSA and FSA plans, subject to plan terms
* Eligibility for critical illness insurance coverage
* 403(b) Retirement plan with employer contribution
* Eligibility for health coverage for legal dependents
* Eligibility for employee/family dental and vision coverage
* Voluntary benefits such as life insurance, long\-term disability, and long\-term care and personal legal services

  


**ABOUT WHATCOM COUNTY:**



Working with Opportunity Council in Whatcom County is more than just a job, it’s a chance to be part of something bigger. Surrounded by the natural beauty of the Pacific Northwest, our community is rooted in compassion, collaboration, and a shared commitment to positive change. Whether you're supporting local families, advocating for equity, or building programs that uplift our neighbors, you'll find purpose and connection here. Join us in building a stronger Whatcom County where everyone has the chance to thrive and contribute

  


*We believe our team should reflect the communities we serve. That’s why we strongly encourage people of color, LGBTQIA\+ individuals, Veterans, and people with disabilities to apply. Opportunity Council is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity employer. We do not permit discrimination based on race, creed, color, national origin, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, religion, marital or family status, pregnancy, military or veteran status, age, genetic information, disability, use of a service animal, or any other status protected by law.*

title: Human Resources Generalist
description:
**Support the People Who Build Our Communities**

  

At MacKay Sposito, we believe great people build great communities, and our Human Resources team plays a vital role in supporting the individuals who make that work possible. We are seeking a thoughtful, service\-oriented Human Resources Generalist to join our growing HR team in Vancouver, WA.

  

In this role, you will serve as a trusted partner to leadership and employees across the organization, with a strong focus on employee relations and training and development. You will help foster a workplace where employees feel heard, supported, and equipped to succeed, while guiding leadership with practical tools and training rooted in our core values of honesty, dedication, and relationships.

  

This is an opportunity to make a meaningful impact in a growing, multi\-state professional services organization. You will help strengthen leadership capability, promote positive employee experiences, and ensure our practices consistently reflect who we are and what we stand for.

  

MacKay Sposito is a rapidly expanding firm with offices in Washington, California, Colorado, New Mexico, and Illinois. As we continue to grow, this role will be instrumental in helping us scale thoughtfully while preserving our culture.

  

If you are energized by building trust, supporting leaders, and delivering people\-first HR solutions that make a lasting difference, we would love to connect.

 **Key Responsibilities**


Employee Relations \& Leadership Support

* Serve as a trusted advisor to leadership and employees on employee relations matters, providing guidance on workplace concerns, conflict resolution, policy interpretation, and performance\-related matters.
* Conduct prompt, thorough, and impartial workplace investigations when appropriate; document findings and recommend appropriate action.
* Coach leadership on best practices related to employee relations, documentation, employee recognition, employee motivation, effective feedback conversations, and overall employee development.
* Partner with leadership to foster a positive, compliant, engaged, and high\-performing work environment.

  

Training \& Development

* Develop, coordinate, and facilitate leadership training programs focused on employee relations best practices, employee development, employee engagement, recognition strategies, and delivering effective feedback.
* Manage and administer training programs, including evaluating and vetting potential Learning Management Systems (LMS); overseeing course selection and development, assignments, completion tracking, reporting, and maintenance of accurate training records.
* Identify training needs in partnership with leadership and recommend development solutions aligned with organizational goals.
* Support onboarding program enhancements to ensure new hires are effectively integrated into the organization.

  

HR Operations \& Compliance (Backup/Shared Support)

* Support the administration and maintenance of the HRIS (Rippling) to ensure data integrity, reporting accuracy, and system efficiency.
* Support benefits administration, including new hire enrollment and life event changes.
* Support the administration of employee leave programs (FMLA, parental leave, and other paid/unpaid leave), ensuring compliance and accurate tracking.
* Conduct exit interviews, analyze trends, and provide insights to leadership.
* Ensure compliance with federal and state employment laws and maintain accurate employee documentation.

 **Qualifications \& Experience:**

* Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field (or equivalent experience)
* Minimum 4 years of progressive HR experience, with strong emphasis in employee relations and employee development
* Demonstrated experience managing employee relations matters, conducting workplace investigations, and coaching managers through complex personnel situations
* Experience developing, facilitating, and evaluating manager training programs, particularly related to employee relations best practices, employee development, and delivering effective employee feedback
* Experience administering and managing a Learning Management System (LMS), including course assignment, tracking completion, reporting, and maintaining training records
* Working knowledge of federal and state employment laws (FMLA, ADA, FLSA, EEOC, etc.)
* Experience supporting employee leave programs and ensuring regulatory compliance
* Hands\-on experience with HRIS platforms, including reporting, data integrity, and system troubleshooting
* Experience supporting employee benefit programs.
* Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple priorities and deadlines
* High level of discretion and ability to maintain confidentiality
* Excellent written and verbal communication skills with the ability to influence and build credibility at all levels of the organization

 **Why join the MacKay Sposito team?**

* A people\-first culture \- We are dedicated to our employees and their families. We want to do what’s best for them, and their well\-being helps drive our business decisions.
* Excellent reputation \- Through our 50\-year history, MacKay Sposito has built and maintained an excellent reputation in our community, and with our clients and business partners. You’ll be joining a team with a strong foundation and reputation that you can be proud to be part of.
* We give back to the community \- MacKay Sposito and our employees believe in giving back to the communities we serve. As a firm, we supported a host of local organizations focusing on our community pillars of veterans and youth. Our team members volunteer as commissioners, committee and board members, youth coaches, and a host of other volunteer positions and initiatives.

 **Our Values:**  

At MacKay Sposito, we’re serious about our work, but we don’t take ourselves too seriously. We have a unique and diverse mix of employees who enjoy each other’s company. In addition to having first\-rate technical skills, we search out people who are friendly, honest and dedicated to their work. We know work isn't only about who you work for, it is also about what you do and how you do it. Led by the strength of our values and a culture shaped by employee ownership, we network with each other, build on each other's contributions, and collaborate to make great things possible.  

  

We believe firmly in the principle of constructive candor – always telling the client everything they need to know to make fully informed decisions. By creating a positive, committed and supportive environment, we’re able to challenge our people to be the best that they can be, and to celebrate every success.  

  

**Who We Are:**  

With more than 50 years of experience, MacKay Sposito is a multidisciplinary firm based in Vancouver, Washington, specializing in public works, energy, and land development. Our passionate team focuses on building lasting relationships through quality work, creative design, and collaboration. We offer civil engineering, land surveying, environmental science and permitting, landscape architecture, and construction management and inspection services.  

  

Salaries are based upon candidate experience and qualifications, as well as various market and business considerations. The wage range for this position is **$32\.00 \- $38\.00** per hour, depending on experience.  

  

Full\-time employees receive a well\-rounded benefits package, including access to our company\-sponsored Medical, Dental, Vision, FSA, HSA, and Supplemental Life Insurance plans. 100% company\-paid Life Insurance, Short Term Disability, and Long Term Disability coverage. Employees are eligible to participate in our company's 401 (k) plan with up to a 4% match

  

Additionally, employees also receive paid vacation time, paid sick time, and 11 paid holidays. MacKay Sposito pays for expenses associated with licensure as well as annual performance bonuses.  

  

Employment requires passing a pre\-employment background check and drug screen.  

We participate in E\-Verify and are proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer.

  

Open until filled.

As the Senior State HR Investigations Consultant for the Chief Human Resources Office (CHRO), you are a leading human resource expert, providing comprehensive advice, guidance, and assistance to State HR professionals on highly complex human resource investigations in state government. The Senior State HR Investigations Consultant conducts comprehensive ongoing evaluations and maintenance of enterprise\-wide HR investigation practices in alignment with the Chief Human Resources Officer, DAS Director and Governor initiatives.
You will serve as a vital expert, developing and delivering training programs, providing strategic consultation to agency leadership on highly complex workplace investigations, and ensuring compliance with applicable laws, policies, and best practices. Most importantly you are responsible for overseeing complex and sensitive investigations involving allegations of misconduct, discrimination, harassment, and other workplace issues while maintaining confidentiality and integrity of investigative processes.
For a full review of the position duties, details, and working conditions, please click here.

title: Director of Human Resources
description:
Hospital Central Services Association (HCSA) is a cooperative laundry owned by the hospitals we serve. HCSA provides quality laundry processing, packroom services and hospital linen distribution services to its owner member hospitals. We accomplish our important work because of the top talent we employ. Our team is unparalleled, and we are looking for an exceptional candidate to join us in the role of Director Human Resources to provide support to our locations in Auburn.

Job Title: Director of Human Resources

**FLSA Classification**: Exempt

**Status**: Full Time

**Reports to**: General Manager

**JOB SUMMARY**

The Director of Human Resources is directly responsible for the overall administration, coordination and evaluation of the human resource and safety functions for the Auburn location. The ideal candidate will promote our company culture and enable business success through the development and management of effective and engaging programs in the areas of recruitment, performance evaluation, employee training and development, employment cycle changes, payroll and benefits and legal and compliance.

**ESSENTIAL DUTIES \& RESPONSIBILITIES**

· Implements human resources strategies by establishing department accountabilities, including talent acquisition, staffing, employment processing, compensation, health and welfare benefits, training and development, records management, safety and health, succession planning, employee relations and retention, legal compliance, and labor relations.

· Identifies legal requirements and government reporting regulations affecting human resources function and ensures policies, procedures, and reporting are in compliance.

· Act as a resource for employees who have questions or concerns about policies, procedures, compliance issues and/or benefits.

· Performing end\-to\-end recruiting for exempt and non\-exempt positions. Partnering with hiring managers to develop recruiting strategies, source and evaluate resumes and complete interviews and reference checks. Process onboarding of new hires.

· Maintain employee records and company HRIS.

· Providing FMLA \& STD forms to eligible employees; tracking of leaves.

· Experience in Employee and Labor Relations including grievances, investigations, and advising supervisors on collective bargaining agreement interpretation.

· Provide assistance to Executive Team during labor negotiations.

· Have a clear understanding of collective bargaining agreement in order to ensure legal compliance. Providing union enrollment applications to eligible employees.

· Assist in processing weekly payroll and reports.

· Implementing and overseeing health and welfare benefits programs (selection, open enrollment, benefit terminations, auditing invoices, communicating with brokers and insurance companies, etc.).

· Knowledge of DOT regulations in order to ensure legal compliance for CDL \-A Drivers.

· Responsible for Safety, OSHA compliance, and Prepare Safety Committee minutes, notes and postings.

· Coordinates, schedules and tracks annual training requirements.

· Point of contact for employee incidents and injuries, assisting in conducting investigations, completing various forms and ensuring they are communicated and processed correctly.

· Complete and manage workers compensation reports as required (OSHA \& State Workers Compensation). Implement and oversee Return\-to\-Work Program.

· Scheduling and tracking of flu and Hepatitis\-B vaccines.

· Assist with planning of company functions.

**MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS**

1\. Education: Bachelor’s degree preferred

2\. Current SPHR/PHR Certification preferred

3\. Direct experience with performance management, total rewards, employee relations, recruiting/hiring, legal/regulatory compliance, training and labor relations required.

4\. Demonstrated initiative and leadership skills.

2\. Minimum of 5 years of comprehensive HR Management experience.

3\. Other required Knowledge, Skills, and Abilities:

· Ability to maintain strict confidentiality in all aspects of the job.

· This position must successfully utilize the Microsoft Office suite of products to include: Word, Outlook, and Excel. Must be able to learn other company databases.

· This is a highly collaborative and communicative position. It requires constant communication with team mates and vendors by email, phone, in\-person and fax. The ability to communicate accurate messages regarding business\-related collaborations, while applying the utmost professionalism and tact, is an essential function of the job. While position requires ability to work cooperatively in a team setting, must also have the ability to perform tasks independently with minimal supervision.

· Excellent communication skills both written and verbal as well as excellent organizational skills.

**SALARY \& BENEFITS**

· Competitive Wages, Salary range: 125K – 180K

· Medical/Dental Benefits

· Paid Holiday \& PTO

· Bonus eligible position

**EQUIPMENT USED**

· MS Office Suite (Word, Excel and Outlook)

· HRIS databases utilized in performing Human Resources functions

· Office Equipment – copier, fax, phone, printer

**MENTAL \& PHYSICAL DEMANDS/WORKING CONDITIONS**

1\. Mental Effort: Ability to question and analyze complex information.

2\. Physical Requirements: Ability to sit for long periods of time.

3\. Working Conditions: General office conditions apply such as moderate temperature and noise\-levels.

Pay: $125,000\.00 \- $180,000\.00 per year

Benefits:

* 401(k)
* Dental insurance
* Health insurance
* Life insurance
* Paid time off
* Vision insurance

Work Location: In person

title: Leaves Coordinator
description:

**Work type:** Officer of Administration  

**Location:** Eugene, OR  

**Categories:** Administrative/Professional, Human Resources  


**Department:** Human Resources  

**Appointment Type and Duration:** Regular, Ongoing

**Compensation Band:** OH\-OA05\-Fiscal Year 2025\-2026  

**FTE:** 1\.0


#### **Application Review Begins**



March 23, 2026; position open until filled


#### **Special Instructions to Applicants**



To ensure consideration, a complete application must include:  

1\. A current resume/CV.  

2\. Three (3\) professional references with contact information. References will not be contacted until you are notified.


#### **Department Summary**



University Human Resources is dedicated to strategically partnering with the university community as a comprehensive, innovative, and results\-oriented department modeled on the very best human resources practices. We strive to foster an inclusive environment that will promote employee success and attract a diverse array of high performing professionals. Working in University Human Resources provides a unique opportunity to promote diversity, equity, and inclusion in our workforce.
  

  

The HR Programs and Services unit manages the university's benefits, medical leaves, work\-life, HR compliance, learning and development, and recognition programs for all employees. The unit provides HR support services to the campus community and is responsible for generating and distributing HR communications to all university employees. The unit develops and maintains HR programs in collaboration with campus stakeholders to support university leadership and institutional priorities.


#### **Position Summary**



The Leaves Coordinator reports to the Associate Director of Benefits and is responsible for educating and advising employees and departments on the various leave options and processes, and provides coordination of the following leaves programs for the University: Family \& Medical Leave Act (FMLA); Oregon Family Leave Act (OFLA); Paid Leave Oregon (PLO); Parental leave; hardship and military leave; and the University’s donated leave program(s).
  

  

The Leaves Coordinator position requires an understanding of state and federal leave laws, university leave programs, as well as consideration of the rights and responsibilities outlined in the Collective Bargaining Agreements, as they relate to medical leaves. The Leaves Coordinator tracks and stays up to date on FMLA, OFLA, PLO and other state and federal laws on a regular basis to assure that the university remains in compliance with all applicable programs.  

  

This position is based in Eugene with some level of flexibility, including the option to work remotely with frequent commuting to campus. All adjusted work arrangements require supervisor and leadership approval.


#### **Minimum Requirements**


* Two years’ experience in Benefits, Leaves Administration, or equivalent combination of education and experience


#### **Professional Competencies**


* Knowledge and understanding of medical leave administration
* Knowledge and understanding of leave administration and compliance
* Familiarity with federal and state laws that affect leaves administration
* Ability to accurately process large volumes of detailed information in a timely manner
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills
* Flexibility to shift directions quickly when priorities change, recommend creative solutions to problems, and deliver against tight deadlines
* Commitment to the highest standards of work product, confidentiality, and integrity
* Effective communication skills; ability to effectively communicate complex information to individuals from diverse backgrounds and cultures
* Strong interpersonal, collaboration, customer service, and teamwork skills
* Empathy in interactions with customers in stressful situations
* Proficiency in using Microsoft Office software and databases


#### **Preferred Qualifications**


* Bachelor’s degree
* Working knowledge of the Banner HRIS system
* Experience working in higher education or the public sector
* Experience working with Collecting Bargaining Agreements


**FLSA Exempt:** No



**All offers of employment are contingent upon successful completion of a background check.**

title: Leave Manager
description:
**Company Description**  

A LITTLE ABOUT US:


At Dungarvin, we are more than a provider of support services; we’re a mission\-driven team rooted in respect, inclusion, and person\-centered care. Since 1976, we’ve been dedicated to enhancing the quality of life for people with intellectual and developmental disabilities, including those with mental health, behavioral, or complex medical needs.


Our mission is simple but powerful: respecting and responding to the choices of people in need of supports. This mission guides everything we do; from creating individualized service plans to building strong partnerships with families, teams, and communities.


As a Dungarvin employee, you’ll be at the heart of that mission. You’ll help people live meaningful lives on their own terms; supporting their goals, honoring their choices, and empowering their independence.


With services nationwide, our team is united by a shared commitment to making a real difference; one person, one voice, one choice at a time.


Join our nationwide family and let us help you achieve your unique goals and dreams.

**WE OFFER:**

* **Compensation:** $ 25\.25/hr Fixed Rate
* **Work Environment**: Remote must reside within the state of **Washington**
* **Team Culture:** You’ll join a supportive team that operates with good intentions, shows up for one another, and works from a positive place while helping each other succeed.
* **Work Schedule:** Typically, 8 AM – 5 PM CST, flexibility available
* **Retirement Savings:** 401 K plan with up to 3% employer match after one year of service.
* **Company\-Provided Hardware:** All necessary equipment will be provided
* **Time Away:** PTO that increases with tenure, PTO donation options, and paid sick leave
* **Insurance \& Health:** Medical, dental, and vision coverage, pet insurance, supplemental insurance options, and flexible spending and health savings accounts (FSA and HSA). Plus, 100% company\-paid long\-term disability and life insurance.
* **Employee Perks:** National Brand Discounts, Employee Referral Program, Employee Assistance Program and access to Tapcheck (get 50% of your pay before payday)
* **Job Mobility:** Dungarvins multi\-state footprint allows internal job mobility nationwide.

  

**Job Description**  

As **Leave Manager** at Dungarvin, you’ll be the go\-to expert for employees and leaders navigating leaves of absence, accommodations, and workers’ compensation. Every case you manage will be handled with care, precision, and compliance, ensuring a positive experience for employees while keeping the organization protected. Reporting to the Sr. Leave Manager, you’ll play a pivotal role in shaping HR processes, supporting employee well\-being, and guiding leaders through complex situations; all while making a meaningful impact on the people and the organization you serve.

**What You Get To Do:**

* Manage all employee leaves of absence in compliance with federal and state regulations, including FMLA
* Serve as a key resource for employees and managers on leave, accommodations, and related processes
* Assist employees with completing required leave paperwork, including explaining benefits and PTO usage
* Coordinate and communicate return\-to\-work information and notifications
* Maintain accurate leave, accommodation, and workers’ compensation documentation and tracking
* Manage workers’ compensation claims and support early return\-to\-work efforts
* Support OSHA compliance, including bloodborne pathogen exposure and incident tracking

  

**Qualifications** **MUST HAVE:**

* **HR Experience**: 2\+ years of human resources experience, with a strong understanding of state and federal employment laws, leave management, and workers’ compensation processes.
* **HRIS Expertise:** Experience with HR or leave management platforms: UKG Pro, Workday, or People Assist to manage HR processes efficiently.
* **Technical Proficiency:** Microsoft Office 365 (Excel, Word, Outlook, PowerPoint), as well as Microsoft Teams and SharePoint, to support reporting, analysis, collaboration, and communication needs.

**NICE TO HAVE:**

* **Education:** Bachelor’s degree related to Human Resources (or equivalent combination of education and experience).
* **Professional Certification:** PHR, SHRM\-CP, or CHRS (Certified Human Resources Specialist).
* **Multi\-State HR Experience:** Experience in multi\-state HR operations or human services environments.
* **Specialized Training:** Completion of specialized certification or formal training in FMLA, leave administration, or related HR compliance areas is a strong plus.

  

**Additional Information** **SKILLS CRITICAL FOR SUCCESS:**

* **HR Experience \& Scale Management:** Proven experience supporting large workforces ( 60\-100 employees) and managing HR processes efficiently.
* **Independent Problem Solving \& Judgment**: Works autonomously, makes sound decisions, and resolves issues effectively by separating facts from emotion.
* **Confidentiality \& Integrity:** Handles sensitive employee and organizational information with the highest level of discretion, ethical standards, and consistent follow\-through.
* **Communication \& Influence:** Clearly and confidently communicates with employees, managers, and external partners, redirecting conversations as needed.
* **Emotional Intelligence \& Professionalism:** Maintains a calm, productive approach in complex or emotionally charged situations.
* **Time Management \& Organization**: Highly organized and proactive, able to prioritize multiple tasks, meet deadlines, and manage competing priorities without letting anything slip through the cracks.
* **Accountability \& Direct Issue Resolution:** Takes ownership from start to finish, following through on commitments and addressing challenges head\-on to move situations forward efficiently.
* **Collaboration \& Teamwork:** Work effectively with HR colleagues, risk management, supervisors, and employees to ensure seamless HR operations.

**WHY YOU’LL LOVE THIS ROLE:**


This is a role where your work matters. You’ll be the trusted go\-to for employees and leaders during some of the most important moments at work, owning cases from start to finish and directly shaping how people experience the organization. You’ll have the autonomy to think on your feet, solve real problems in real time, and help improve processes that actually make life easier, all while being part of a supportive, values\-driven HR team that trusts you to do your job well without micromanagement. If you thrive on variety, accountability, and doing work that truly makes an impact, this role will be incredibly rewarding.

**IMPORTANT INFORMATION:**

***At Dungarvin, diversity and inclusion are a part of what makes our organization strong. Together, we can continue to work towards an inclusive culture that supports our employees and persons served. Compass and Dungarvin are affirmative action and equal opportunity employers.***


The deadline for internal applications is set for two weeks from the date of the job posting.

title: Director, People Partnering – AI Infrastructure (US)
description:
**About Nscale**
----------------



Nscale is the GPU cloud engineered for AI. We provide cost\-effective, high\-performance infrastructure for AI start\-ups and large enterprise customers. Nscale enables AI\-focused companies to achieve superior results by reducing the complexity of AI development. Our GPU cloud bolsters technical capabilities and directly supports strategic business outcomes, including cost management, rapid innovation, and environmental responsibility.



We thrive on a culture of relentless innovation, ownership, and accountability, where every team member takes pride in their work and drives it with excellence and urgency. As an Nscaler, you'll build trust through openness and transparency, where everyone is inspired to do their best work. If you join our team, you'll be contributing to building the technology that powers the future.


**About the Role**
------------------



The Director, People Partnering – AI Infrastructure (US) will lead the development and delivery of strategic people partnering across Nscale's AI Infrastructure organisation in the United States.



This role will directly partner with the **President of AI Infrastructure** and their leadership team across **Technology, Product, AI Infrastructure Operations, and Security**. You will play a critical role in shaping the people strategy that enables the build, scale, and secure operation of Nscale's AI infrastructure platform.



This is a highly strategic and commercially aligned role, balancing organisational design, talent strategy, leadership capability, and operational excellence within a deeply technical, high\-growth environment.



Reporting to the Global Director, Head of People Partnering, you will act as a trusted advisor to executive leadership while ensuring scalable, compliant, and high\-performance people practices across the US region.


**What You'll Be Doing**
------------------------


### **Strategic Executive Partnering**


* Serve as a trusted advisor to the President of AI Infrastructure and their senior leadership team across Technology, Product, AI Infrastructure Operations, and Security.
* Shape and execute a forward\-looking people strategy aligned to AI Infrastructure growth, operational resilience, and innovation priorities.
* Coach senior leaders to build high\-performing, accountable, and scalable teams in a complex, technical environment.
* Partner on organisational design, workforce planning, and capability mapping to support rapid infrastructure expansion and product evolution.  

Act as the People lead for AI Infrastructure within the global People Partnering leadership team.

### **Organisational Design \& Workforce Planning**


* Lead organisational design initiatives to ensure scalable structures, clear accountabilities, and optimal team effectiveness across engineering, product, operations, and security.
* Drive workforce planning aligned to infrastructure buildout, capacity expansion, and security requirements.
* Partner with leaders to identify critical skills (e.g., distributed systems, AI workloads, GPU infrastructure, cloud security) and develop targeted hiring and development strategies.
* Support succession planning and leadership pipeline development for critical technical roles.

### **Performance \& Leadership Enablement**


* Lead performance enablement frameworks that drive clarity, accountability, and excellence across technical teams.
* Design leadership development initiatives tailored to engineering and infrastructure leaders.
* Provide executive coaching to senior leaders to elevate capability, influence, and alignment with Nscale values.
* Equip managers with tools and training to foster continuous learning, innovation, and high standards of delivery.

### **Employee Relations \& Risk**


* Provide expert guidance on complex employee relations matters within the US regulatory environment.
* Partner closely with Legal and Security leadership on sensitive cases involving technical teams or operational risk.
* Ensure compliance with US employment laws and regulatory standards.
* Develop ER strategies that balance speed, fairness, and risk mitigation in a fast\-scaling infrastructure environment.

  


### **Change \& Transformation**


* Lead change management initiatives tied to infrastructure scaling, platform evolution, security enhancements, and operating model changes.
* Develop communication and engagement strategies to drive clarity and adoption across technical populations.
* Balance business velocity with employee experience and organisational health.

### **Talent Strategy \& Capability Building**


* Partner with Talent Acquisition to build proactive hiring strategies for niche technical and AI infrastructure skillsets.
* Identify capability gaps across Technology, Product, Operations, and Security and implement targeted development initiatives.
* Support internal mobility and career architecture for technical career paths.

Strengthen retention strategies for high\-impact technical and infrastructure talent.  
* 

### **People Data \& Analytics**


* Leverage people analytics to assess trends in attrition, engagement, performance, and workforce capability within AI Infrastructure.
* Establish dashboards and KPIs specific to technical and infrastructure functions.
* Translate workforce data into actionable insights for executive decision\-making.

### **Compensation \& Rewards**


* Partner with Global Total Rewards to ensure competitive compensation structures aligned to US technical markets.
* Guide leadership on reward strategies for critical infrastructure and security talent.
* Monitor market trends in AI infrastructure, cloud engineering, and security talent to maintain competitive positioning.

**About You**
-------------


* Extensive HR leadership experience within high\-growth technology, cloud, infrastructure, or AI\-driven environments.
* Proven experience partnering directly with C\-suite or executive\-level technical leaders.
* Strong understanding of engineering, product, infrastructure operations, and security team dynamics.
* Experience supporting US\-based technical populations in scaling environments.
* Strong expertise in organisational design, workforce planning, employee relations, and leadership development.

Deep knowledge of US employment law and regulatory frameworks.  
* 
* High commercial acumen and ability to align people strategy with infrastructure growth and operational performance.
* Strong analytical capability, with experience leveraging data to influence executive decision\-making.
* Exceptional stakeholder management, influencing, and executive coaching skills.
* High emotional intelligence, adaptability, and ability to operate in ambiguity.

**Preferred**
-------------


* Experience supporting AI infrastructure, cloud, hyperscale, or data centre organisations.
* Experience in a matrixed, globally distributed technology organisation.
* Membership of a recognised HR professional body.
* Master's degree in Human Resources, Organisational Psychology, Business Administration, or related field.

*For information on how Nscale handles candidate personal data, please see our Employee \& Candidate Privacy Notice:* *Here.*

title: Human Resources - Human Resources Coordinator
description:
Summary
  
The HR Coordinator provides administrative support to the Human Resources function across multiple U.S. locations. This role assists with onboarding, HR systems data entry, employee documentation, benefits administration support, and compliance tracking. The position works closely with the HR Director, HR Business Partner, and Payroll to support day\-to\-day HR operations. This role is an excellent opportunity for an early\-career HR professional to gain hands\-on experience across multiple HR functions, including HRIS administration within Dayforce, employee lifecycle processes, and HR compliance support.
  
Essential Functions
  
* Assist with onboarding, including offer letters, new hire paperwork, background checks, and HRIS data entry.
* Process employee status changes in HRIS, such as job changes, compensation updates, and terminations.
* Maintain employee files and HR records in compliance with federal and state regulations, including I\-9s.
* Support offboarding activities, including separation documentation and unemployment claim preparation.
* Track leaves of absence, accommodations, training, and other compliance\-related items.
* Help employees with benefits questions and coordinate with benefits vendors.
* Provide basic payroll support, including auditing data and assisting with payroll inquiries.
* Support HR reporting, including headcount, workforce, and compliance reports.
* Respond to general HR inbox inquiries and route complex matters to HRBP or HR Director.
* Assist with scheduling meetings, investigations, and documentation preparation.


Knowledge Skills and Abilities
  
* Minimum: High school diploma or equivalent.
* Preferred: Associate’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field.
* 1–3 years of HR administrative or coordinator experience.
* Equivalent combination of education and experience also considered.
* Experience with HRIS systems (Dayforce preferred)
* Knowledge of basic federal and state employment laws
* Strong attention to detail and organizational skills
* Ability to manage multiple tasks and meet deadlines
* Proficient in Microsoft Office Suite, with strong Excel skills and experience supporting HR reporting.


Working Conditions
  
Standard office environment, on a hybrid policy with mandatory in office days.
